# Dolby OptiView Documentation

> Developer documentation for Dolby OptiView streaming products.
> URL: https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/

## Important: Product Name Changes (Rebranding)

All products below were rebranded under the "Dolby OptiView" umbrella. Legacy names still appear in code, npm packages, API references, CSS class names, and older documentation pages. The old and new names refer to the same products.

| Current Name | Former Name(s) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Dolby OptiView Player | THEOplayer | npm: `theoplayer`, JS class: `THEOplayer.Player`, CSS: `.theoplayer-container` |
| Dolby OptiView Ads | THEOads | Server-Guided Ad Insertion (SGAI) service |
| Dolby OptiView Live Streaming | THEOlive | Low-latency HESP streaming; config key: `theoLive`, source: `TheoLiveSource` |
| Dolby OptiView Real-time Streaming | Millicast, Dolby.io Streaming | WebRTC-based sub-500ms latency; dashboard: https://streaming.dolby.io |
| Open Video UI | (same) | Open-source UI components for OptiView Player (formerly THEOplayer) |
| Dolby OptiView Ad Engine | (same) | Just-in-time ad conformance |

Note: The GitHub organization is still named "THEOplayer", npm packages use "theoplayer", and many code-level identifiers retain the old naming. When searching for documentation or code, try both the old and new names.

## Products

- [OptiView Player](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/theoplayer/): Multi-platform video player SDK (Web, Android, iOS, Roku, React Native, Flutter)
- [Open Video UI](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/open-video-ui/): Open-source UI component library for OptiView Player enabling complete customization of the player experience
- [OptiView Ads](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/ads/): an end-to-end Server-Guided Ad Insertion and Server-Side Ad Insertion solution for live streaming content
- [OptiView Ad Engine](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/ad-engine/): Instant virtualized or just-in-time ad creative QC, conformance/transcoding, and packaging.  mp4, mov, vast, hls (any to any conversion). Includes support for Dolby Vision and Dolby Atmos encoding. Can be used for VOD transcoding/transformation in addition to ads.
- [OptiView Live](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/theolive/): High quality live video streaming.  Consistent latency with a operator settable latency from 2 to 10 seconds to keep viewers in syncLow-latency live streaming (HESP protocol) and HLS support.
- [OptiView Real-time Streaming](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/millicast/): WebRTC real-time streaming (<500ms latency) for interactive use-cases.

- [OptiView Ads Getting Started](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/ads/)
- [OptiView Live Getting Started](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/theolive/)

- [React Native Connectors](https://optiview.dolby.com/docs/theoplayer/connectors/react-native/)

## Documentation

- [Dolby OptiView Ad Engine](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine.md): The OptiView Ad Engine is a just-in-time, serverless conformance service that transforms ads from their original master format into a streaming format that matches the primary content stream. This ensures smooth, consistent ad playback, improving the overall viewing experience and enhancing audience satisfaction. Because the Ad Engine operates just-in-time, it can prepare ads for playback within the span of an Early Ad Break Notification (EABN). The Ad Engine is compatible with any ad server, streaming engine, or player. Beyond transforming ads, it can also be used to integrate other types of content, such as editorial segments.
- [Ad Conformance](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/ad-conformance.md): The OptiView Ad Engine can just-in-time convert your ad from a master format into your streaming delivery format. Ads are often sourced from master media files such as high bitrate MP4 or MOV files. Sometimes the master media files is referenced in a VAST (Video Ad Serving Template) file. Or, the ad may actually already be in an HLS format, but you need to convert it to a different bitrate ladder. On the delivery side, you may want to create a single MP4 file or a multi-bitrate HLS file. You may want to create an HLS, but have that HLS be referenced in a VAST file. All of these different types of conformance can be executed by the OptiView Ad Engine:
- [Ad Delivery](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/ad-delivery.md): You have multiple ways that your ad can be delivered as part of the ad conformance process. The OptiView Ad Engine can directly act as the origin, or you can have the Ad Engine place the finished files into your own origin (e.g. in your cloud or on your CDN).
- [Ad Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/ad-profile-creation.md): Before any ads can be processed by the OptiView Ad Engine, you must create one or more ad profiles. The purpose of an ad profile is to define the parameters of your encoding, such as resolution, bitrate, etc. This is also where you define the audio normalization you desire and any quality control checks that should be performed.
- [ABR Audio Assignment](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/audio-assignment.md): You can create multiple audio bitrates in HLS, and then assign different bitrates to different video renditions. For example, on your 1080p high bitrate video you may have audio at 128kbps, whereas on your small 360p video you may only want audio at 64kbps. This allows you to minimize the combined video/audio bitrate for certain streams.
- [Audio Normalization](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/audio-normalization.md): An area of importance for ad processing is to have the ads match the source content in terms of audio volume. It will drive away viewers if your ads have wildly different volume than your primary content. Whether by accident or intent, ads are often set to very high volume. You can use the OptiView Ad Engine to normalize these ads so that all your ads are (1) consistent and (2) match your primary content.
- [Cloud Preview](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/cloud-preview.md): There is typically a significant difference between high-quality media masters and content intended for streaming. Media masters are not only extremely high bitrate, but are often stored in formats (for example, QuickTime ProRes) that are not streamable.
- [Image Generation](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/image.md): The Image API provides a simple way to extract a single image frame from a media asset stored in the cloud. It is commonly used to generate images without downloading or transcoding the full media file.
- [Media Info](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/media-info.md): The Media Info endpoint provides a fast, normalized view of the technical characteristics of a media asset stored in the cloud. The Media Info API inspects a media file and returns a structured JSON response describing the container, duration, bitrate, and all detected tracks (video, audio, timecode, and others).
- [Ad Quality Control](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/quality-control.md): One of the realities of ads is that there are many instances where the ad source can be problematic. These types of problems can include things like too long duration, too short duration, too small dimensions, too low of a bitrate, etc. A bad ad can look bad, sound bad, or simply stall your player, so it is important to verify the quality of the ad.
- [Up-Converting Ads to Dolby Vision and Atmos](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/how-to/upconvert-to-vision-atmos.md): The vast majority of ads are produced in SDR (Standard Dynamic Range) video and stereo audio. Many premium VOD and live assets are delivered, however, in Dolby Vision and Atmos. Dolby Vision is the leading HDR (High Dynamic Range) format and Dolby Atmos is the leading object-oriented audio format. You can optimize the viewing experience for your customers by up-converting the ad video and audio to match the primary content. Converting the ad video to Dolby Vision gives the ad more color depth and makes it look stunning. And most importantly, viewers are not switching back and forth between HDR primary content and SDR ads.
- [Cloud Preview - Codemill](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/cloud-preview-codemill.md): Generates Accurate Player templates for instant cloud asset media playback.
- [Cloud Preview - HLS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/cloud-preview-hls.md): Generates HLS Preview templates for instant cloud asset media playback.
- [Cloud Preview - Omakase](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/cloud-preview-omakase.md): Generates Omakase Player Session templates for instant cloud asset media playback.
- [Conform Ad](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/conform-ad.md): Conforms a single ad to a specified encoding profile.
- [Conform Ad Pod](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/conform-ad-pod.md): Conforms multiple ads (an ad pod) to a specified encoding profile.
- [Create Ad Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/create-ad-profile.md): Creates an ad encoding profile.
- [Delete Ad Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/delete-ad-profile.md): Deletes a specific ad profile.
- [Dolby OptiView Ad Engine](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/dolby-optiview-ad-engine.md)
- [Get Ad Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/get-ad-profile.md): Requests the properties of a specific ad profile.
- [Image](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/image.md): Image
- [Get All Ad Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/list-ad-profiles.md): Gets a list of all current ad profiles.
- [Media Info](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/media-info.md): Media Info
- [Update Ad Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ad-engine/reference/update-ad-profile.md): Updates an ad encoding profile.
- [Dolby OptiView Ads Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads.md): OptiView Ads is an ad insertion service for LIVE content (VoD support coming soon), utilizing Server-Guided Ad Insertion (SGAI). On these pages, you'll learn how to get started with OptiView Ads, how to configure the player and integrate the signaling service APIs.
- [API references](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api.md)
- [CreateMonetizedStream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/create-monetized-stream.md): Creates and deploys a new monetized stream in the environment, resulting in a deployment of a signaling service.
- [CreateMonetizedStreamBreak](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/create-monetized-stream-break.md): Creates an ad break on an existing monetized stream (signaling service).
- [CreateMonetizedStreamOverlay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/create-monetized-stream-overlay.md): Creates an overlay on an existing monetized stream (signaling service).
- [DeleteMonetizedStream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/delete-monetized-stream.md): Deletes a single monetized stream (signaling service) based on its identifier.
- [DeleteMonetizedStreamBreaks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/delete-monetized-stream-breaks.md): Deletes an adbreak linked to the monetized sctream
- [DeleteMonetizedStreamOverlay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/delete-monetized-stream-overlay.md): Deletes an overlay linked to the monetized sctream
- [GetMonetizedStream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-stream.md): Retrieves a single monetized stream (signaling service) based on its identifier, along with its configuration details.
- [GetMonetizedStreamBreaks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-stream-breaks.md): Retrieves all adbreaks linked to the monetized sctream
- [GetMonetizedStreamBreaksPaginated](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-stream-breaks-paginated.md): Retrieves all adbreaks linked to the monetized stream
- [GetMonetizedStreamOverlays](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-stream-overlays.md): Retrieves all overlays linked to the monetized sctream
- [GetMonetizedStreamOverlaysPaginated](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-stream-overlays-paginated.md): Retrieves all overlays linked to the monetized sctream
- [GetMonetizedStreams](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-streams.md): Retrieves all created and deployed monetized streams (signaling services) along with their configurations.
- [GetMonetizedStreamsPaginated](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/get-monetized-streams-paginated.md): GetMonetizedStreamsPaginated
- [THEOads Api](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/theoads-api.md): The THEOads REST API allows you to create and manage monetized streams, which are deployments of signaling services. Each monetized stream corresponds to an origin stream that is monetized using THEOads (SGAI). For detailed information on the various parameters and their usage, please refer to the getting started guide.
- [UpdateMonetizedStream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/update-monetized-stream.md): Updates a single monetized stream (signaling service) based on its identifier, along with its configuration update.
- [UpdateMonetizedStreamBreaks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/update-monetized-stream-breaks.md): Patches an adbreak linked to the monetized sctream
- [UpdateMonetizedStreamLayout](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/api/signaling/update-monetized-stream-layout.md): Updates the default layout for an existing monetized stream (signaling service).
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started.md): These guides provide the steps required to get started with OptiView Ads. They cover the deployment and API integration of the Signaling Service into your existing workflow, as well as the integration of OptiView Ads into your application using various OptiView Player SDKs.
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads on Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/android.md): This guide will get you started to integrate OptiView Ads in your OptiView Player Android SDK: configure the license, update dependencies and set the source description.
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads on Chromecast CAF](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/chromecast.md): This guide will get you started with OptiView Ads using the OptiView Player Chromecast CAF SDK.
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads on iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/ios.md): This guide will get you started to integrate OptiView Ads in your OptiView Player iOS SDK: configure the license, update dependencies and set the source description.
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads on React Native](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/react-native.md): This guide will get you started with OptiView Ads in your OptiView Player React Native SDK: configure the license, update dependencies and set the source description.
- [Signaling service](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/signaling-service.md): The Signaling Service is provided as a service and can be integrated into any existing content management workflow through its APIs, enabling seamless adoption without significant changes to your current setup.
- [Getting started with OptiView Ads on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/getting-started/web.md): This guide will get you started with OptiView Ads in your OptiView Player Web SDK: configure the license, update dependencies and set the source description.
- [How-to guides](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides.md): These guides provide detailed information on specific features of OptiView Ads (SGAI). They cover how ad impressions are tracked, how custom ad tag parameters can be included in the ad decisioning request, how ad formats can be overridden client-side, and how breaks can be signaled.
- [Ad impression tracking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/ad-impressions.md): When inserting an ad experience using OptiView Ads, the player sends a beacon to OptiView's server to track the ad impression usage. An ad impression refers to each ad experience inserted into the content and presented to the viewer using OptiView Ads, counted from the first presentation of the experience.
- [Ad targeting parameters](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/ad-tag-parameters.md): With the Google DAI Pod Serving, there is the possibility to pass a limited set of ad targeting parameters on your stream request to personalize your streams.
- [Create backdrop images](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/create-backdrop-layout.md): A backdrop is an companion image that is shown along with the player during an ad.
- [Dynamic backdrops](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/dynamic-backdrops.md): Some layouts, such as L-shape and Double Box, make use of a backdrop. These can be specified on both the monetized stream and the ad break in 2 ways:
- [Custom GAM Creatives](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/gam-custom-creatives.md): When using GAM for delivering dynamic backdrops or overlays respectively through the backdropURIGamProperties or gamProperties properties, you need to first configure corresponding Creatives in the GAM console.
- [Override ad experience layout](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/override-layout.md): OptiView Ads is able to insert ad breaks into your content via the signaling server. The layout that is used while playing is usually determined by the signaling server. Currently, you can have the following layout modes :
- [Scheduling breaks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/scheduling-breaks.md): OptiView Ads can insert ad breaks into your content, but it requires placement information to do so. This page will outline the various options for scheduling ad breaks.
- [Scheduling overlays](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/scheduling-overlays.md): OptiView Ads can insert overlays into your content, but it requires placement information to do so. This page will outline the various options for scheduling overlays.
- [Workflow integration](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/how-to-guides/workflow-integration.md): For implementing OptiView Ads, the Signaling Service must be integrated within your existing video workflow. This service acts as an intermediary between the media origin and the CDN, enabling ad insertion and metadata enrichment.
- [Limitations and known issues](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/ads/limitations.md): This page lists the current limitations and known issues of OptiView Ads.
- [Contribution Guidelines](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing.md): These pages provide guidelines and templates on how to write documentation for Dolby OptiView products.
- [FAQ - Guidelines](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/guidelines/faq.md): FAQ articles answer a question a customer (might) ask on a certain limitation, behavior or platform.
- [How-to-Guides - Guidelines](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/guidelines/how-to-guides.md): How-To-Guides explain how to use/implement a certain API/feature/SDK.
- [Knowledge Base - Guidelines](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/guidelines/knowledge-base.md): A General Information article provides information on a certain concept or general, industry topic.
- [Title of the Article](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/templates/faq.md): This is a short answer to the title.
- [Title of the Article](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/templates/how-to-guides.md): The first paragraph of the body describes
- [Title of the Article](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/templates/knowledge-base.md): Introduction
- [Tips and Tricks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/tips-and-tricks.md): This article will provide some dos and don'ts and some general information on how to write documentation in Markdown.
- [Writing documentation: a step-by-step guide](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/contributing/writing-documentation.md): This article will guide you through writing new or updating an existing documentation article, submitting your changes
- [OptiView Real-time Streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming APIs were created to make it easier to stream your high-value content at scale with ultra-low latency.
- [Streaming Dashboard](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/about-dash.md): The OptiView Real-time Streaming Dashboard enables you to easily provision tokens, broadcast streams, and manage account settings.
- [Analytics APIs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/analytics-api.md): The Analytics APIs allow you to query your usage independent of the OptiView Real-time Streaming Dashboard and get details of how your users are consuming your streams.
- [Media Stats](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/analytics-api/media-stats.md): Both the Live Broadcaster and Hosted Viewer provide a Media Stats option to open a modal overlay during broadcast or playback that displays information that can be helpful in judging the performance or diagnosing issues when troubleshooting.
- [Create Storage Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-create-storage-profile.md): Create a storage profile identifying the configuration for a cloud storage provider that can receive media assets when they are finished processing. Only one storage profile will be set as a default for the entire account, otherwise you may specify a storage location using the `storageProfileId` on any individual media clip request.
- [Delete Storage Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-delete-storage-profile.md): Specify the id of a storage profile you wish to delete.
- [Read Account Distribution Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-get-account-distribution.md): Gets account wide distribution settings. Whenever publish tokens are created, these will be the default settings used for features that impact how streams are distributed.
- [Read Account Security Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-get-account-security.md): Gets account wide security settings. If a Token (either Publish or Subscribe) does not define any security settings, the account wide rules are used.
- [Get Account Media Assets Expiration Rule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-get-expiration-rules.md): Get account level expiration rule for various media asset types.
- [Read Account Geo Cascade Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-get-geo-cascade.md): **Please refer to [Get Account Distribution Settings](#tag/Account/operation/Account_GetAccountDistribution)**
- [Get Storage Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-get-storage-profile.md): Get details of a saved storage profile
- [List Storage Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-list-storage-profiles.md): List all storage profiles created on your account.
- [Update Account Distribution Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-update-account-distribution.md): Update account wide distribution settings to enable/disable the feature or update the account default cluster list. Whenever publish tokens are created, these will be the default settings used for features that impact how streams are distributed.
- [Update Account Security Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-update-account-security.md): Update account wide security settings. Updated to an empty array `[]` removes all rules of that type.
- [Update Account Media Assets Expiration Rules](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-update-expiration-rules.md): Update account level expiration rule for various media asset types.
- [Update Account Geo Cascade Settings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-update-geo-cascade.md): **Please refer to [Update Account Distribution Settings](#tag/Account/operation/Account_UpdateAccountDistribution)**
- [Update Storage Profile](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-update-storage-profile.md): Change the details of a storage profile.
- [Validate Third Party Storage Setup](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/account-validate-third-party-storage.md): Validates third party storage is configured with permissions for Dolby.io to upload media assets.
- [Account Geo](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-account-geo.md): Get bandwidth and views per country code on account over a date range.
- [Account Geo Series](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-account-geo-series.md): Get bandwidth, duration, publishes, and views as a time series per country code on account over a date range.
- [Account Geo Total](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-account-geo-total.md): Get bandwidth and duration per country code on account over a date range.
- [Account Series](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-account-series.md): Get usage as a time series on account over a date range.
- [Account Total](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-account-total.md): Get total usage on account over a date range.
- [DRM Daily Usage](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-drm-daily-usage.md): Get DRM daily license requests and their status over a date range.
- [DRM Total Usage](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-drm-total-usage.md): Get DRM total license requests and their status over a date range.
- [Series bandwidth for streams specified by TrackingID](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-series-bandwidth-for-tracking-id.md): Get bandwidth information  used as a time series on specified TrackingIDs over a date range on a stream-by-stream basis. Response keys are requested TrackingIDs, followed by keys for ISO 8601 dates from requested
- [Get Transcoder Minutes Series](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-series-transcoder-minutes-for-account.md): List Transcoder uptime in minutes calculated between two date ranges for an account. The data will show a list of dates each with corresponding transcoder minutes for that specific day.
- [Total bandwidth for streams specified by TrackingID](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-total-bandwidth-for-tracking-id.md): Get total bandwidth for TrackingIDs specified on a stream-by-stream basis. Response keys are requested TrackingIDs.
- [Get Total Transcoder Minutes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-total-transcoder-minutes-for-account.md): Lists total transcoder uptime in minutes calculated between two date ranges for an account.
- [Series bandwidth per TrackingID per stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-tracking-series-for-streams.md): Get bandwidth used as a time series on specified streamnames over a date range. Response keys are requested streamnames, followed by keys for ISO 8601 dates from requested
- [Total bandwidth per TrackingID per stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-get-tracking-total-for-streams.md): Get total bandwidth for each TrackingID associated with specified streamNames over a date range. Response keys are requested streamnames.
- [Usage](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-media-asset-usage.md): Returns the total number of bytes used to store all media assets. The total is inclusive of all contributing media types including recordings, clips, and timelines.
- [Usage Billable](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-media-asset-usage-billable.md): Returns the number of gigabyte hours of storage used for a given date range. The total is inclusive of all contributing media types including recordings, clips, and timelines.
- [Streams Geo](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-streams-geo.md): Get bandwidth and views per country code on specified streamnames over a date range.
- [Streams Geo Series](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-streams-geo-series.md): Get bandwidth, duration, publishes, and views as a time series per country code on specified streamnames over a date range.
- [Streams Geo Total](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-streams-geo-total.md): Get bandwidth and duration per country code on specified streamnames over a date range.
- [Streams Series](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-streams-series.md): Get usage as a time series on specified streamnames over a date range.
- [Streams Total](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/analytics-streams-total.md): Get total usage on specified streamnames over a date range.
- [Read Clusters](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/cluster-get-clusters-info.md): Default cluster and list of available clusters.
- [Update Cluster](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/cluster-update-cluster-info.md): Update default cluster on account.
- [Director API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/director-api.md)
- [Publish](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/director-publish.md): Request for url and authorization to publish a stream.
- [Subscribe](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/director-subscribe.md): Request for url and authorization to subscribe to a stream.
- [DrmLicence_GetDrmLicence](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/drm-licence-get-drm-licence.md): DrmLicence_GetDrmLicence
- [DrmLicence_GetFairplayCertificate](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/drm-licence-get-fairplay-certificate.md): DrmLicence_GetFairplayCertificate
- [Configuration](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/multi-track-video-configuration.md): Supplies multi track video configuration to OBS. Please refer to OBS for request schema. Supports OBS schema versions: [2024-06-04, 2025-01-25].
- [WHEP](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/whep-whep-subscribe.md): WHEP endpoint for subscribers. Request body is SDP Offer. Response body will contain SDP Answer. Location header contains remote WHEP endpoint url.
- [WHIP](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/director/whip-whip-publish.md): WHIP endpoint for publishers. Request body is SDP Offer. Response body will contain SDP Answer. Location header contains remote WHIP endpoint url.
- [Get Account Encoder Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/encoder-profile-get-account-encoder-profiles.md): Get encoder profiles associated with an account
- [Read Account Geo Restrictions](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/geo-geo.md): **Please refer to [Get Account Security Settings](#tag/Account/operation/Account_GetAccountSecurity)**
- [Update Account Geo Restrictions](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/geo-update-geo.md): **Please refer to [Update Account Security Settings](#tag/Account/operation/Account_UpdateAccountSecurity)**
- [Create Media Asset](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-create-media-asset.md): Create a live clip from an ongoing live stream. You must provide the stream name and start time, and once processing is complete the media asset of the type clip will be available for retrieval.
- [Delete Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-delete-media-assets.md): Deletes multiple media assets from storage. Only media assets with status Complete or Error can be deleted.
- [Delete All Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-delete-media-assets-2.md): Deletes all media assets of a specified type from storage.
- [Generate Upload Credentials](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-get-upload-credentials.md): Generate AWS access credentials for uploading media to a specified S3 location within the Dolby system.
- [List Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-list-media-assets.md): List media assets, excluding those that have been deleted.
- [Read Media Asset](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-read-media-asset.md): Gets media asset specified by id. Includes temporary download link and a CDN served link if the media asset is complete.
- [Register Media Asset](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-register-media-asset.md): Register media asset upon completion of content upload. Call this after generating credentials to upload and then uploading your media. This will cause our service to scan the upload location for completed uploads.
- [Update Media Asset](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-assets-update-media-asset.md): Update media asset settings. <i>Note: This will not make any updates related to the media itself such as adding or updating the media files.</i>
- [Create Media Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-create-media-distribution.md): Creates a new media distribution for viewing HLS media assets.
- [Create a new security key for a media distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-create-security-key.md): Create a new security key for a media distribution. Each media distribution may have up to 2 security keys.
- [Delete Media Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-delete-media-distribution.md): Deletes media distribution specified by a mediaDistributionId.
- [Delete a media distribution security key](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-delete-security-key.md): Delete a media distribution security key
- [Get all security keys of a media distribution by ID](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-get-security-keys.md): Get all security keys of a media distribution by ID
- [List Media Distributions](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-list-media-distribution.md): List all media distributions with specific sorting and pagination.
- [Read Media Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-read-media-distribution.md): Gets media distribution specified by mediaDistributionId.
- [Update Media Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/media-distributions-update-media-distribution.md): Update media distribution settings.
- [Millicast API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/millicast-api.md): # Introduction
- [Stream Details](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/monitoring-get-stream.md): Get stream details by stream name.
- [Stream Summary](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/monitoring-get-stream-summary.md): Get stream summary by stream name. This is a lite version of [Stream Details](#tag/Monitoring/operation/Monitoring_GetStream) which does not include feed details.
- [List Streams](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/monitoring-list-recent-streams.md): List all streams created within last hour with specific sorting and pagination. If the transcoderId is provided, all streams associated with the current or last active instance will be returned, regardless of the stream creation time. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [List Stream Summary](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/monitoring-list-stream-summary.md): List a summary of all streams created within last hour with specific sorting and pagination. If the transcoderId is provided, all streams associated with the current or last active instance will be returned, regardless of the stream creation time. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering. This is a lite version of [List Streams](#tag/Monitoring/operation/Monitoring_ListRecentStreams) which does not include feed details.
- [Create Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-create-token.md): Creates new token given a label and associated stream name(s). Stream names are limited to 128 characters.
- [Delete Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-delete-token.md): Deletes token specified by the token's ID. The Token ID can be found using the List Tokens API or in the API response of Create Token API.
- [Disable Publish Token(s)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-disable-tokens.md): Disables Publish Token(s) by their Token ID. An array of Token IDs can be used for bulk disable.
- [Get active Publish Token ID](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-get-active-token-by-stream-id.md): Gets the Publish Token ID of an active stream by its Stream ID. Publish Tokens for long running streams published before 1st October 2022 will not be returned. If you need to stop a long running stream, use the Dashboard or try republishing the stream.
- [Get all active Publish Token IDs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-get-all-active-tokens-by-account.md): Get all Publish Token IDs for active streams on the account. Publish Tokens for long running streams published before 1st October 2022 will not be returned. If you need to stop a long running stream, use the Dashboard or try republishing the stream.
- [List Tokens](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-list-tokens.md): List all tokens with specific sorting and pagination. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [List Tokens By Cluster](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-list-tokens-by-cluster.md): List all tokens with specific sorting and pagination that matches given cluster region. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [List Tokens By Name](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-list-tokens-by-name.md): List all tokens with specific sorting and pagination that matches given token name or stream name. Tokens with wildcard stream names are excluded from the responses. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [Read Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-read-token.md): Gets token specified by token id.
- [Sync restreams](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-sync-restream.md): Apply any re-stream configuration changes for running streams immediately.
- [Update Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-1-update-token.md): Update token stream information as well as updates token itself.
- [Update Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/publish-token-v-2-update-token.md): Update token stream information as well as updates token itself.
- [Create Clip](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-create-record-clip.md): **Please refer to [Create Media Asset](#operation/MediaAssets_CreateMediaAsset)**
- [Delete All Files](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-all-record-files.md): **Please refer to [Delete All Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets2)**
- [Delete Clip Request](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-clip-request-live.md): **There is no direct replacement for this endpoint. Media assets can be deleted using [Delete Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets).**
- [Delete Clip Sources](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-clip-sources.md): **Please refer to [Delete Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets)**
- [Delete Clip Source Expiry Rule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-expiry-rule.md): **Please refer to [Update Account Media Assets Expiration Rules](#operation/Account_UpdateExpirationRules)**
- [Delete File](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-record-file.md): **Please refer to [Delete Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets)**
- [Delete Files](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-delete-record-files.md): **Please refer to [Delete Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets)**
- [Get Clip Request](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-get-clip-request.md): **There is no direct replacement for this endpoint. Media assets can be retrieved using [Get Media Asset](#operation/MediaAssets_ReadMediaAsset).**
- [Get Clip Source Expiry Rule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-get-expiry-rule.md): **Please refer to [Get Account Media Assets Expiration Rules](#operation/Account_GetExpirationRules)**
- [Read File](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-get-record-file.md): **Please refer to [Read Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ReadMediaAsset)**
- [List Available Clip Sources](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-list-available-clip-sources.md): **Please refer to [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets) and query with `type=timeline`**
- [List Clip Requests](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-list-clip-requests.md): **There is no direct replacement for this endpoint. Media assets can be retrieved using [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets).**
- [List Files](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-list-record-files.md): **Please refer to [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets)**
- [List Files By Stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-list-record-files-by-stream.md): **Please refer to [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets) and query using the `streamName`parameter**
- [List Files By Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-list-record-files-by-token.md): **Please refer to [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets) and query using the `tokenId` parameter**
- [Usage](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-record-file-usage.md): **Please refer to [Usage](#operation/Analytics_MediaAssetUsage)**
- [Usage Billable](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-record-file-usage-billable.md): **Please refer to [Usage Billable](#operation/Analytics_MediaAssetUsageBillable)**
- [Update Clip Source Expiry Rule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-update-expiry-rule.md): **Please refer to [Update Account Media Assets Expiration Rules](#operation/Account_UpdateExpirationRules)**
- [Delete Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-v-2-delete-media-assets.md): **Please refer to [Delete Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_DeleteMediaAssets)**
- [List Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-v-2-list-media-assets.md): **Please refer to [List Media Assets](#operation/MediaAssets_ListMediaAssets)**
- [Read Media Asset](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-v-2-read-media-asset.md): **Please refer to [Read Media Asset](#operation/MediaAssets_ReadMediaAsset)**
- [Validate Third Party Storage Setup](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/record-files-validate-storage-profile.md): **Please refer to [Validate Third Party Storage Setup](#operation/Account_ValidateThirdPartyStorage)**
- [Advanced Analytics API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/reporting/advanced-analytics-api.md)
- [Viewer Records](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/reporting/records-get-viewer-records.md): Get range of viewer connection records with optional start/stop times. Also optionally including ongoing connections.
- [Reprioritize an active stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/stream-reprioritize-stream.md): Update stream priority specified in token and restart stream to re-assign priority.
- [Stop all active streams](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/stream-stop-by-account.md): Stop all currently active streams associated with your account
- [Stop an active stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/stream-stop-stream.md): Stop an active stream by its Stream ID
- [Create Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-create-token.md): Creates new token given a label and associated stream name(s). Stream names are limited to 128 characters.
- [Delete Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-delete-token.md): Deletes token specified by the token's ID. The Token ID can be found using the List Tokens API or in the API response of Create Token API.
- [List Tokens](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-list-tokens.md): List all tokens with specific sorting and pagination. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [List Tokens By Name](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-list-tokens-by-name.md): List all tokens with specific sorting and pagination that matches given token name or stream name. Tokens with wildcard stream names are excluded from the responses. If response array is empty, you have reached the end of the list ordering.
- [Read Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-read-token.md): Gets token specified by token id.
- [Update Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-1-update-token.md): Update token stream information as well as updates token itself.
- [Update Token](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/subscribe-token-v-2-update-token.md): Update token stream information as well as updates token itself.
- [Trigger Thumbnail](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/thumbnails-trigger-thumbnail.md): Immediately generate a thumbnail for active streams. Requests are limited to 6 per minute per stream.
- [Create Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-create-transcoder.md): Create a Cloud Transcoder to enable Adaptive Bitrate (ABR) Simulcast streams without requiring multiple broadcast contribution sources.
- [Delete Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-delete-transcoder.md): Delete the configuration of a Transcoder and any instances for it for the given transcoderId. If the Transcoder is still running it will be shutdown immediately.
- [Get Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-get-transcoder.md): Returns the configuration of a Transcoder and any individual host instances for the given transcoderId.
- [Get Transcoder Instance](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-get-transcoder-instance.md): Get transcoder instance information by Transcoder Instance ID. Instances can be listed and filtered using the List Transcoder Instances endpoint.
- [List Transcoder Instances](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-list-transcoder-instances.md): A Transcoder Instance is an individual host within a cluster that has been configured based on the Transcoder settings. By default, Deleted instances are returned unless specified with Status parameter. This can be used to get historical instance information.
- [List Transcoder Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-list-transcoder-profiles.md): A Transcoder Profile is a pre-defined configuration for how to direct the bitrate ladder in distributing individual layers.
- [List Transcoders](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-list-transcoders.md): Return the Transcoders that have been configured for your account. You can use query parameters to filter, sort, or paginate the results returned.
- [Create Transcoder Schedule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-create-schedule.md): Creates a Schedule for Transcoders specified.
- [Delete Transcoder Schedule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-delete-schedule.md): Deletes a Transcoder schedule by its Schedule ID. Schedule ID can be found from Create/Get Transcoder Schedule response.
- [Get Transcoder Schedule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-get-schedule-by-schedule-id.md): Gets schedule summary by Schedule ID.
- [Get Schedules By Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-get-schedules-for-transcoder.md): Get all Schedules for a single transcoder.
- [List Transcoder Schedules](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-list-all-schedules.md): Lists all Transcoder Schedules. Sort by Created Date by default. Able to be filtered by Schedule Status.
- [Update Transcoder Schedule](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-scheduler-update-schedule.md): Updates a Transcoder Schedule by its Schedule ID.
- [Start Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-start-transcoder.md): Enable a Transcoder by ID to accept incoming broadcast sources.
- [Stop Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-stop-transcoder.md): Disable a Transcoder by ID so that it will shutdown and no longer accept incoming broadcast sources.
- [Update Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/transcoder-update-transcoder.md): Make changes to the configuration of an existing Transcoder. Changes should take effect without requiring a server restart except for DNS prefix and cluster, which can only be updated when the transcoder is in a shutdown state.
- [Add Webhook](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-add-webhook.md): Add new webhook on account.
- [Read Webhook](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-get.md): Webhook url and details used on account.
- [List Webhooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-list-webhooks.md): Iterative call to list webhooks on account.
- [Remove Webhook](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-remove-webhook.md): Removes all webhook data for account.
- [Test Webhook](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-test-webhook.md): Sends a test event for the specified webhook type.
- [Update Webhook](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/api/webhooks-update-webhook.md): Update webhook url, events to signal, or refresh signing secret.
- [Broadcast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast.md): Guide to Encoding Content for Broadcasting Streams
- [Multi-Source Broadcasting](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/multi-source-broadcasting.md): Multi-source Broadcasting allows you to ingest and send additional capture sources, such as different camera angles, to the OptiView Real-time Streaming platform where they will be logically grouped into an optimized multi-view experience over a single WebRTC connection.
- [NDI](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/ndi.md): NDI® Tools is a free suite of applications designed to introduce you to the world of IP. NDI makes it possible to connect to any device, in any location, anywhere in the world – and transmit live video to wherever you are. NDI systems and sources on your network. Combine NDI with OptiView Real-time Streaming to deliver real-time video for remote or interactive experiences.
- [Publishing Parameters](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/publishing-parameters.md): Broadcast URL Parameters allow you to customize the behavior of an individual source feed by modifying the URL used of specific sources when establishing a connection.
- [Redundant Ingest](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/redundant-ingest.md): To provide a resilient and reliable streaming experience, the OptiView Real-time Streaming platform allows broadcasting multiple redundant contribution streams. This solution ensures seamless content delivery to viewers, even in the case of unexpected technical issues.
- [High Availability Management](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/redundant-ingest/high-availability-management.md): Note: To make use of this feature, please be familiar with the concept of Redundant Ingest
- [RTMP | RTMPS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/rtmp-and-rtmps.md): Real-Time Messaging Protocol (RTMP) has been well supported by popular hardware and software applications for many years. In addition to WebRTC, OptiView Real-time Streaming allows you to broadcast and distribute content from an RTMP or secure RTMPS source.
- [RTSP](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/rtsp.md): Real-Time Streaming Protocol (RTSP), is a network control protocol used for controlling the delivery of multimedia data, such as video and audio files, over IP networks. OptiView Real-time Streaming supports RTSP to facilitate seamless integration with existing infrastructure and workflows that rely on this protocol for streaming IP camera feeds.
- [SRT](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/srt.md): Secure Reliable Transport (SRT) is an open-source protocol that uses an intelligent packet retransmit mechanism on top of a UDP data flow, along with AES-128 and 256-bit encryption. OptiView Real-time Streaming natively supports publishing from an SRT source.
- [Ingest Raw Frames](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/using-native-sdk-ingest-raw-frames.md): Ingesting raw video and audio frames can be useful for creating and managing unique WebRTC encoding workflows. Whilst traditional encoders provide general-purpose encoding solutions, there are some use cases such as real-time streaming from drones where traditional encoding solutions are too heavy, power consumptive, or expensive to suffice.
- [WebRTC | WHIP](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/broadcast/webrtc-and-whip.md): Web Real-Time Communication (WebRTC) is an open-source project that defines secure voice, audio, and data delivery between peers using web browsers or native clients. OptiView Real-time Streaming allows you to broadcast and distribute content using WebRTC for real-time global streaming to large-scale audiences.
- [Capture](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture.md): To broadcast content, you either need a physical camera capturing the content, a virtual camera from a game engine, or a source of content available via other content creation tools such as OBS or Adobe Premiere.
- [360 VR Cameras](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/360-vr-cameras.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming allows for 360-degree Real-time broadcasts.
- [Action Cameras](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/action-cameras.md): Sports and action cameras are designed to capture fast movements or provide a point-of-view perspective while in motion. Many of these types of cameras support WebRTC or RTMP output that can be used as a broadcast streaming source.
- [Camera Drones](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/live-streaming-from-drones.md): Using a drone, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), or other flying cameras can be a way to provide a unique perspective on any real-time activities. Many of these remote-controlled devices can support network connectivity that enables either direct WebRTC or RTMP output that can be used as a broadcast streaming source.
- [Professional Cameras](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/production-cameras.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming supports broadcast-grade workflows that use professional cameras and encoders to deliver high-quality, real-time video and audio streams globally to massive audiences. Broadcast-grade cameras are typically recommended for sports streaming, live events, and experiences where quality and low latency are prioritized. This guide will outline how to prepare professional cameras for capture so you can broadcast with OptiView Real-time Streaming.
- [Screensharing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/screensharing.md): Screensharing or screen capturing is an important feature for virtual events, webinars, and many other types of broadcasts that involve sharing information. This guide will outline three options for managing screen capture in a way that is compatible with OptiView Real-time Streaming:
- [Web Cameras](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/capture/web-cameras.md): Web cameras are an easy and accessible way to capture content for broadcast with OptiView Real-time Streaming. Since web cameras are ubiquitous, there are many different software solutions to capture from a web camera.
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog.md): Find out what's new.
- [Dashboard Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-dolbyio-dashboard.md): Updates to the Dolby OptiView Real-time Dashboard and Applications.
- [Platform and Media Server Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-dolbyio-platform-media-server.md): Updates to Dolby OptiView's Real-time Streaming Platform and Media Server.
- [Native SDK Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-native-sdk.md): Updates to the Native SDK for Android, iOS, macOS, Linux, and Windows applications.
- [OBS WebRTC Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-obs-webrtc.md): Releases of the open-source WebRTC fork of Open Broadcaster Software (OBS).
- [REST APIs Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-rest-apis.md): Updates to REST APIs, and Webhooks.
- [Web SDK Changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/changelog/changelog-web-platform.md): Updates to the JavaScript Web SDK for custom web applications, React Native applications, and the VueJS Web Viewer Plugin.
- [Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution.md): The OptiView Real-time Content Delivery Network (CDN) offers a range of server-side features that users can toggle and adjust via the REST APIs or your account Dashboard to ensure streams are secure, stable, and scalable.
- [Access Control](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/access-control.md): Stream security is a priority for the OptiView Real-time platform. Along with stream protocol encryption, OptiView Real-time Streaming provides a number of different server-side features to help protect both broadcasting (Publishing) a stream and viewing (Subscribing) to a stream, all managed via their respective tokens.
- [Allowed Origins](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/access-control/allowed-origins.md): Impose limits on who may use a token to broadcast or view a stream by defining rules based on the domain name or IP address in which the request originated. Allowed Origins enables defining specific domain names or sub-domain patterns that any request must originate from. Similarly, with IP Address Filters you can define rules that limit access by specifying the network IP address.
- [Geo-blocking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/access-control/geo-blocking.md): Geo-blocking refers to the action of restricting access to certain content based on the geographical location of the user. Filtering can be allowed or denied by countries as well as IP addresses. Geo-blocking enables content providers to adhere to specific licensing agreements and distribution rights, protect copyrighted material, or service another layer of privacy when working on private content.
- [Cloud Transcoder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/cloud-transcoder.md): The Cloud Transcoders offered by the Dolby Millicast are responsible for ingesting (SRT or RTMP) and encoding contribution feeds into adaptive-bitrate (ABR) streaming renditions for WebRTC delivery. By adjusting the compression level, resolution, and quality of a video stream to match bandwidth availability it is possible to create an improved viewing experience. The adaptability ensures smoother playback but also conserves bandwidth, making the cloud transcoder an efficient and cost-effective solution optimized for ultra-low latency during the encoding process.
- [DRM](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/drm.md): Digital Rights Management (DRM) is a security mechanism that helps protect premium content or intellectual property within a stream from being shared without permission. This mechanism prevents bad actors from screen sharing, taking screenshots, and other unauthorized distribution.
- [Multi-Region Support](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/multi-region-support.md): The WebRTC Content Delivery Network (CDN) refers to geographically distributed clusters of servers which work together to provide real-time streaming worldwide at scale. OptiView Real-time Streaming distributes streaming content across multiple regions through a network of cloud hosting providers.
- [Geo-cascading](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/multi-region-support/geo-cascading.md): Geo-cascading is a key component of content delivery networks (CDNs) and streaming platforms that aim to provide a high-quality streaming experience for users around the world. To reduce latency and improve reliability, geo-cascading uses a network of clusters that are strategically placed in various geographical locations. This way, viewers can receive streams from regions that are the closest to their location.
- [Re-streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/re-streaming.md): Re-streaming enables you to distribute your broadcasts to additional RTMP(S) and SRT output destinations while simultaneously providing real-time WebRTC playback. This can be useful for distribution to other B2B media partners or for extending reach to larger audiences on public consumer social media platforms.
- [Stream Recordings](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings.md): With _Media Assets_ you can keep a copy of a real-time stream where media can be used for a variety of use cases such as video on demand (VOD) playback, archiving and auditing, event highlights and instant replay, etc. To support this variety of purposes there are options for the type of media assets that you will create from a stream. Here are the different types of media assets on the platform:
- [How-to Get Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings/how-to-get-media-assets.md): Media assets are media recordings that are created one of several ways:
- [How-to Setup Storage Profiles](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings/how-to-setup-media-storage.md): With Live Clipping you can have clips stored directly to your preferred third-party cloud storage provider. This gives you additional control over storage costs and manage usage of media clips.
- [How-to Upload Media Assets](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings/how-to-upload-media-assets.md): You can upload your own media assets to OptiView storage to be played back via the OptiView CDN. These are files that are pre-transcoded before upload for distribution and are served over Dolby's CDN and can be a single asset such as an MP4 or a multi-file asset such as an HLS asset that has a series of manifests and media files.
- [How-to Start Live Clipping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings/live-clipping.md): Live Clipping allows you to capture one or more simultaneous media files (ie. clips) while a broadcast event is in progress. This is in contrast to a recording which is a media file that captures the full duration of a single source only after the completion of a broadcast. The duration of a clip can be customized to capture highlights or shorter segments while still live for use cases that want instant replay, social sharing, or audit reviews.
- [How-to Start Recording](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/stream-recordings/start-recording.md): You can keep a recording of a full broadcast and download it to share or provide as video on demand. The recording will be made available shortly after the end of a broadcast. If you want a recording of a live event while it is in progress, you should learn more about Live Clipping feature.
- [Syndication](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/syndication.md): With OptiView Real-time Streaming you can syndicate content through multiple distribution partners or channels. This can be helpful to maximize the number of viewers or monetizing content through other platforms. In order to accomplish this, it is important to be able to track syndicated streams for analytics and billing.
- [Simulcast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/distribution/using-webrtc-simulcast.md): Simulcast is a mechanism for distributing streaming content with variations that are optimized for viewers who are under different network conditions or device resolutions. By using a mechanism of WebRTC to distribute content, multiple sources will be available simultaneously that have been encoded with different bitrates and resolutions (HD, SD, LD).
- [Getting started with OptiView Real-time Streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/getting-started.md): Dolby OptiView's Real-time Streaming makes it easier to globally stream your high-value content with broadcast-quality picture and sound to massive audiences all with subsecond latency. Use this tutorial to quickly set up and run your own private stream and begin building applications for a variety of use cases.
- [2. Create a Streaming Web App](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/getting-started/creating-real-time-streaming-web-app.md): In Part 1 we reviewed how-to log into your OptiView Real-time Streaming Dashboard, create a publish token, start a broadcast, and playback that broadcast in the hosted web viewer or in an iframe, all without writing any code. We'll continue getting started by substituting the broadcast and playback components with a custom-built web application.
- [4. Create Mobile Streaming Apps](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/getting-started/mobile-apps.md): Dolby Millicast provides Native SDKs for both Android and iOS to speed up development of mobile applications with real-time streaming. Follow the guides below to add playback or broadcasting capabilities for your target platforms.
- [3. Automate using REST APIs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/getting-started/using-rest-apis.md): The OptiView Real-time Streaming REST API allows remote management of your OptiView Real-time Streaming account. To enable remote access you must first use the Token system to securely authenticate your applications with OptiView Real-time Streaming. To acquire an API Token, log into your OptiView Real-time Streaming account and follow the directions here: acquiring your API token.
- [1. Use the Dashboard](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/getting-started/using-the-dashboard.md): This part explains how to get started using just the OptiView Real-time Streaming Dashboard to both broadcast and playback a real-time stream without writing any code.
- [Hardware Encoders](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders.md): Hardware-based Encoders for Broadcasting Real-time Streaming Content
- [Elgato](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders/elgato.md): Elgato is a world-leading provider of audiovisual technology for content creators in streaming platforms. Focused on revolutionizing creative workflows, Elgato has a growing ecosystem with cross-compatibility and seamless user experience.
- [Haivision](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders/haivision.md): Haivision provides professional broadcasting equipment to the streaming industry. The KB series of H.264 & HEVC Internet Media Encoders and Transcoders deliver high-quality video streaming for resolutions up to 4K, available as small form factor portable appliances as well as rack-mountable enterprise-grade servers. The KB Series provides you with multiple options for live event streaming, helping you deliver the highest quality live video to your global internet audience.
- [Magewell](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders/magewell.md): Magewell is a leading encoder manufacturer that supports connecting to the OptiView Real-time Streaming Service for broadcasting real-time streams. This guide outlines a number of different options for broadcasting streams from a Magewell device to the OptiView Real-time servers.
- [Osprey](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders/osprey.md): Osprey Video is a world leader in broadcasting solutions and an early adopter of WHIP for their Osprey Talon 4K-SC Encoder. By supporting WHIP, Osprey is able to offer an encoder that can natively encode WebRTC streams at broadcast-level quality for a variety of applications, such as sports broadcasting, live event broadcasting, and auction streaming.
- [Teradek](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/hardware-encoders/teradek.md): Teradek is a designer and manufacturer of professional high-end video equipment and solutions for creating and sharing content. Teradek offers a range of support for different broadcast standards and protocols, including support for RTMP, RTMPS, SRT, and NDI, all of which can be distributed via the OptiView Real-time CDN.
- [Live Broadcast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/how-to-broadcast-in-dashboard.md): The Live broadcast section of the Streaming Dashboard is the place to manage all of your Publish Tokens. You use these tokens to securely broadcast. You can also launch a hosted Broadcaster web app to start a broadcast.
- [Live Monitoring](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/live-monitoring.md): The Live monitoring section of the Streaming Dashboard is the place to view all of your active streams. This page displays all active ingest streams and streams that have been offline for the last hour. At a glance, you can check the number of connected viewers, stream duration, and status of your streams. Live monitoring allows you to access information about your streams to quickly diagnose and triage any issues.
- [Publish Tokens](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/managing-your-tokens.md): To broadcast a real-time stream the connection must be authenticated with a valid publish token. The OptiView Real-time platform uses token authentication for publishing to prevent unauthorized distribution of streaming content from your OptiView Real-time account. Without a valid token, publish requests will be rejected by the servers.
- [Multi-source Builder](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/multi-source-builder.md): The Multi-source tab of the Streaming Dashboard is a tool to help you build and configure multiple publishing sources to enable simulcast or power a multi-view experience. You can add multiple SRT, RTMP or WebRTC sources and use the user-interface to configure multi-bitrate publishing. The Multi-source tab also includes a URL to launch the hosted player with the multi-viewer configuration, customizable `` code to embed the hosted player into your webpage, and the ability to export your multi-source configuration as a file.
- [Platform Requirements](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/network-requirements.md): Codecs
- [STUN and TURN Service](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/platform-requirements/stun-and-turn-service.md): What is STUN and TURN?
- [Update to Project and TrackIds](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/platform-requirements/update-to-project-and-trackids.md): This is part of our Multi-view feature and only impacts users who where using the multiview feature and their own player SDK implementation. If you are using the hosted video player, there is no update to apply.
- [Playback](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback.md): The final stage of the streaming workflow is taking the stream and playing it back for the end user. Similar to broadcasting, decoding the stream for playback is supported with a viewer for a wide range of devices and environments.
- [Audience Engagement](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/audience-engagement.md): PubNub
- [Audio Multiplexing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/audio-multiplexing.md): The OptiView Real-time platform supports Audio Multiplexing, a feature that allows viewers to receive multiple audio streams in a conference-like experience, where each audio stream is emphasized or deemphasized based on activity.
- [Client Analytics](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/client-analytics.md): With Client Analytics, we are referring to any metrics or KPIs that help provide a better understanding of the end-user Quality of Experience (QoE). This becomes critical when there are playback issues to troubleshoot or for establishing alerts when monitoring rules meet a particular threshold.
- [Datadog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/client-analytics/datadog.md): Datadog is a tool for monitoring application performance measurements and user engagement.
- [Datazoom](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/client-analytics/datazoom.md): Datazoom enables the capture of streaming quality analytics, measurement of user engagement, tracking of content consumption, and understanding Quality of Experience (QoE) metrics that occur during playback.
- [Amino](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/devices/amino.md): Amino.tv is a leading provider of media and entertainment technology solutions, specializing in IPTV and hybrid TV devices that deliver content across various platforms. Known for their high-quality set-top boxes and software, Amino empowers service providers to offer seamless streaming experiences. The Amino H200 stands out as a high-performance, flexible set top box supporting the latest streaming protocols and standards. Additionally, Amino's Orchestrate platform offers powerful fleet management, with centralized control and monitoring for efficient and reliable operations across large deployments.
- [Frame Metadata](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/frame-metadata.md): In addition to streaming audio and video there are many use cases that require additional metadata about what is happening in the stream. We refer to this as Frame Metadata which allows for embedding and extraction of custom application data that has frame-level accuracy.
- [Stream Preview](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/hosted-player/customization.md): When having a stream, it is possible to set up a loading screen before your event goes live. Having a stream-loading screen is a valuable asset as it serves as a polished introduction to the streaming experience, presenting a seamless and professional image to viewers. Beyond its aesthetic appeal, a Stream preview maintains the audience's engagement during critical moments, as it can serve to provide a buffer for brief technical adjustments or seamless transitions between scenes.
- [Hosted Player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/hosted-viewer.md): The Hosted Player refers to the streaming video player that can be used as a standalone web application or embedded into a website to meet the needs of many workflows without requiring extensive software development.
- [IPv6 Support](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/ipv6-support.md): IPv6 is a communications protocol used by some ISP and cellular networks. In most cases end users will have either an IPv4 or IPv4 + IPv6 network connection. If you have viewers that only have IPv6 connectivity, then you will need to opt-in to IPv6 support with Millicast. This document describes how to opt-in and how IPv6 interacts with other features of the Millicast service.
- [Multi-view](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/multiview.md): Multi-view lets you ingest and render multiple OptiView Real-time video and audio streams simultaneously inside a browser or mobile native applications. Once rendered, you can switch seamlessly between streams, allowing you to control how you view the content. By giving viewers content control, broadcasters can enable real-time experiences and engagement that leave viewers wanting more.
- [Players and SDKs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks.md): Dolby OptiView Streaming offers Client SDKs for customers who prefer creating their own applications. The SDKs can help you to connect, capture, publish, and view streams from the Streaming Platform. The platform offers the JavaScript SDK, React Native plugin, Flutter SDK, and Native SDK that supports creating Android, iOS, Mac, Windows, and Linux applications.
- [Getting started with THEOPlayer for Millicast on Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/player.md): Usage
- [Native viewer sample app for Android and Android TV devices](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/samples/sample-apps-android-viewer.md): The application available in this repository demonstrates the capabilities of the OptiView Real-time Streaming monitoring experience, built for Android and Android TV devices.
- [Millicast SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/sdk.md): The Native SDK for creating Android applications. You may use it in your Android project to connect, capture, publish, or subscribe to streams using the OptiView Real-time Streaming Platform.
- [Getting Started with Publishing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/sdk/getting-started-with-publishing.md): Follow these steps to add the publishing capability to your application.
- [Getting Started with Subscribing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/sdk/getting-started-with-subscribing.md): Follow these steps to add the subscribing capability to your application.
- [How-to Add Picture in Picture](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/sdk/how-to-add-picture-in-picture.md): Picture-in-Picture (PiP) was introduced with Android 8.0 (API level 26). This feature allows you to support a floating video frame that persists even when the user sends the application to the background or switches to another application.
- [Migration Guide for Android SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/android/sdk/sdk-migration-guide.md): This migration guide provides some tips for upgrading or downgrading between releases of the Android SDK.
- [Desktop](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/desktop.md): The Native SDK provides C++ APIs for desktop platforms, such as Linux, Mac, and Windows. You may use the SDK in your project to connect, capture, publish, or subscribe to streams using the OptiView Real-time Streaming Platform.
