- [Building Test Applications](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/desktop/building-test-applications.md): The Native SDK offers simple test applications for checking whether the SDK installation is correct. The following instructions explain how to build the applications.
- [Getting Started with Publishing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/desktop/getting-started-with-publishing.md): Follow these steps to add the publishing capability to your application.
- [Getting Started with Subscribing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/desktop/getting-started-with-subscribing.md): Follow these steps to add the subscribing capability to your application.
- [Third-Party Dependencies](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/desktop/third-party-dependencies.md): All Native SDKs depend on the dynamic Network Device Interface (NDI) library. Although the application does not interface with the NDI library directly, it is a runtime requirement of the Millicast SDK. Windows and Linux builds also depend on the dynamic FFmpeg library. The location of the additional dynamic libraries depends on the platform:
- [Flutter](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/flutter.md): This SDK is no longer being developed. If you would like to use a Flutter SDK with OptiView Real-Time (Millicast) please contact OptiView Sales.
- [Getting started with Millicast for iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/player.md): Usage
- [Native viewer sample app for iOS, iPad OS, and tvOS devices](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/samples/sample-apps-ios-viewer.md): The application available in this repository demonstrates the capabilities of the OptiView Real-time Streaming monitoring experience, built for iOS, iPad OS, and tvOS devices.
- [Millicast SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/sdk.md): The Native SDK for creating iOS and tvOS applications. You may use it to connect, capture, publish, or subscribe to streams using the OptiView Real-time Streaming Platform.
- [Getting Started with Publishing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/sdk/getting-started-with-publishing.md): Follow these steps to add the publishing capability to your application.
- [Getting Started with Subscribing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/sdk/getting-started-with-subscribing.md): Follow these steps to add the subscribing capability to your application.
- [How-to Add Picture in Picture](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/sdk/how-to-add-picture-in-picture.md): Picture-in-Picture (PiP) is compatible with iOS 16+. This feature allows you to support a floating video frame that persists even when the user sends the application to the background or switches to another application.
- [Migration Guide for iOS SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/ios/sdk/sdk-migration-guide.md): This migration guide provides some tips for upgrading or downgrading between releases of the iOS SDK.
- [React Native](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/react-native.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming APIs support using the Web SDK with React Native WebRTC to support creating iOS, Android, AndroidTV, and tvOS applications.
- [Broadcast Events](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/viewer-events.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming supports broadcastEvents, sometimes called viewer events, which allow you to add functionality that triggers when various events occur during the stream. This functionality can be helpful for detecting active feeds, changes in simulcast layers, or even the viewer count of a stream.
- [Getting started with THEOPlayer for Millicast on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/web/player.md): Usage
- [Frame metadata with Millicast on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/web/player/frame-metadata.md): In addition to streaming audio and video, Millicast also supports inserting additional metadata about what is happening
- [Publisher and viewer sample app for react](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/web/samples/sample-apps-react.md): The application available in this repository demonstrates the capabilities of the OptiView Real-time Streaming solution for browser applications, built using React.
- [Millicast Web SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/players-sdks/web/sdk.md): Dolby OptiView Player (formerly THEOplayer) now supports real-time streaming (formerly Millicast). Unless directed by your onboarding team, please use the Dolby OptiView Player and not the Millicast SDKs.
- [Securing Stream Playback](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/securing-stream-playback.md): Securing the playback of your Broadcast is one of the most important features provided by the OptiView Real-time platform. Although securing your stream is optional, it is highly recommended for broadcasts that are non-public or paywalled.
- [Source and Layer Selection](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/playback/source-and-layer-selection.md): OptiView Real-time Streaming supports ingesting Multi-source Streams and rendering multiple audio and video streams for building Multi-view and Audio Multiplexing experiences.
- [Software Encoders](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders.md): A software encoder can take raw video frames and convert it into a digital format that is compatible for distribution with OptiView Real-time Streaming. This type of integration can typically be done without additional hardware requirements and eases adoption of real-time streaming workflows.
- [FFmpeg](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/ffmpeg.md): FFmpeg is a free open-source software project with command-line tools for handling video, audio, and other multimedia. It is common practice to use Ffmpeg in production workflows when broadcasting from a media file on disk.
- [Drastic Technologies](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/flowcaster.md): Drastic Technologies' FlowCaster is a tool that enables cloud-based media workflows. FlowCaster makes it simple to use OptiView Real-time Streaming and the WHIP (WebRTC HTTP Ingest Protocol) with no additional software or hardware requirements.
- [GStreamer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/gstreamer.md): GStreamer is a free open-source software project and multimedia framework to build media processing pipelines that support complex workflows. You may want to broadcast over WebRTC from a file on disk or another Real-time Streaming Protocol (RTSP). You can originate the broadcast through GStreamer that ingests the stream utilizing WHIP or forwards with WHEP.
- [LiveU](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/liveu-studio.md): LiveU Studio is a cloud production studio for live video that supports WebRTC streaming to the OptiView Real-time platform with WHIP. This guide outlines how to create and broadcast a high-quality low-latency stream globally with OptiView Real-time Streaming.
- [OBS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/obs.md): Open Broadcaster Software (OBS) is a free open-source software created for broadcasting and recording on your desktop. You can take advantage of this tool to stream high-quality video to your viewers in real time using the OptiView Real-time Streaming service.
- [Enhanced Broadcasting in OBS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/obs/enhanced-broadcasting-multitrack-video.md): This feature is still under development and may be updated, changed, or deprecated without notice. We welcome your feedback. For the moment, "Additional Canvases" in OBS is ignored.
- [Softvelum](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/softvelum.md): Softvelum provides software solutions for streaming media on mobile devices such as iOS and Android. There is support for both WHIP and SRT workflows.
- [vMix](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/vmix.md): vMix is a desktop application that provides a software vision mixer and encoder for the Windows operating system. It allows users to switch inputs, mix audio, record output, and live stream cameras, video files, audio, and more.
- [Zoom](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/software-encoders/zoom.md): Zoom is a video collaboration tool for hosting conferences and meetings. Zoom supports streaming to consumer platforms or any RTMP source such as distributing Real-time Streaming content with OptiView Real-time Streaming.
- [Subscribe Tokens](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/subscribe-tokens.md): Unlike publishing a broadcast, the subscriber (viewer), by default, does not need a token to view a stream. However, if you want to secure your feed from being viewed by non-authenticated users, OptiView Real-time Streaming provides the ability to use subscribe tokens.
- [Token API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/token-api.md): The Token API is a REST endpoint that allows you to automate the creation and management of tokens for your applications. You can create, read, update, and delete publish tokens and subscribe tokens that are used for authorizations with a broadcast stream.
- [Videon](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/videon.md): Videon is a leading video streaming company offering innovative solutions to enhance content delivery and quality. Their flagship product, EdgeCaster, optimizes video streaming with low-latency and high-quality playback across platforms, empowering service providers to deliver seamless and engaging viewing experiences.
- [Diagnostics for Millicast on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/web-player-diagnostics.md): For troubleshooting and analytics purposes, some statistics and diagnostics from Millicast are exposed on the player.
- [Webhooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks.md): Webhooks are callbacks triggered by the platform to notify your application when an event occurs. As an alternative to polling solutions built with the REST API, you can build asynchronous services and integrations that react only when real-time notifications are pushed from the OptiView Real-time platform to your application.
- [Feeds Hooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks/feeds.md): The feeds type of webhook allows you to receive an activity feed for platform events such as when a stream has started or stopped publishing.
- [Media Hooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks/media.md): The media type of webhook allows you to receive a series of notifications for when a recording or live clip changes status. This can be helpful for when you want to automate the download or notifications for when media becomes available.
- [Thumbnail Hooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks/thumbnail.md): The thumbnail type of webhook allows you to receive a static image that is captured every 30 seconds during an active stream. This thumbnail would allow somebody to preview what was on a stream at various intervals.
- [Transcoder Hooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks/transcoder.md): The transcoder type of webhook allows you to receive a state transition notification for platform events such as when a transcoder starts, stops, or is deleted.
- [ViewerConnection Hooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/millicast/webhooks/viewerconnection.md): The viewerConnection type of webhook allows you to receive a series of notifications for when a viewer attempts to connect to a stream or alert when there are no longer viewers watching the stream. This can be helpful for when you want to automate starting and stopping your encoder when a viewer connects.
- [Open Video UI](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui.md): <DocCardList
- [Open Video UI for Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android.md)
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/changelog.md): Find out what's new in Open Video UI.
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/getting-started.md): Start building your UI in just a few minutes!
- [How-to guides](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/guides.md)
- [Setting up Chromecast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/guides/chromecast.md): The Open Video UI for Android has integrated support for Chromecast.
- [Making a custom UI](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/guides/custom-ui.md): Although the default UI was designed to support a variety of usage scenarios, you may still run into a case that it doesn't handle very well. Perhaps you want to move some buttons around, or add like and dislike buttons to the control bar, or perhaps integrate a text chat component inside your player. In these situations, you may want to build a custom player UI to create a truly unique experience for your viewers.
- [Localization](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/android/guides/localization.md): The Open Video UI for Android can be localized to different languages,

- [Open Video UI for Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/web.md)
- [@theoplayer/web-ui](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/web/changelog.md): Find out what's new in Open Video UI.
- [Examples](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/web/examples.md)
- [Advertisements](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/open-video-ui/web/examples/ads.md): The default UI has full support for advertisements.

- [Search the documentation](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/search.md)
- [OptiView live streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive.md): OptiView live is a streaming solution perfect for use cases such as live sports, sports betting and interactivity, with support for all kinds of platforms and devices.
- [OptiView Live REST API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api.md): OptiView Live provides a REST API to manage channels, receive events, create reports, and set up webhooks.
- [Authentication](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/authentication.md): To make secure calls to the REST API, both authentication and authorization are required. OptiView Live uses token–secret pairs to authorize API requests and ensure secure access.
- [Create a new channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/create-channel.md): Create a new channel

- [Get detailed engine monitoring data for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-engines-monitoring.md): Returns channel metadata along with detailed monitoring data for all engines belonging to the channel.
- [Get ingests of a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-ingests.md): Get ingests of a channel
- [Get detailed ingest monitoring data for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-ingests-monitoring.md): Returns channel metadata along with detailed monitoring data for all ingests belonging to the channel.
- [Get browser and OS insights for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-insights-browsers-os.md): Data is only available for the last 30 days.
- [Get country insights for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-insights-countries.md): Data is only available for the last 30 days.
- [Get viewer count insights for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-insights-viewers.md): Data is only available for the last 30 days.
- [Get monitoring data for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-monitoring.md): Returns monitoring data for a specific channel including status of ingests, engines, and distributions.
- [Get engine runs of a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-runs.md): Get engine runs of a channel
- [Get transcoding minutes analytics for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-transcoding-minutes.md): Get transcoding minutes analytics for a channel

- [Get viewing minutes grouped by browser for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-channel-viewing-minutes-browsers.md): Get viewing minutes grouped by browser for a channel

- [Get an ingest by ID](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-ingest.md): Get an ingest by ID

- [Get monitoring data for an ingest](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/get-ingest-monitoring.md): Returns monitoring data for a specific ingest including time-series metrics like packet loss, RTT, and bitrate.

- [Pagination](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/pagination.md): All list endpoints in the OptiView Live API use cursor-based pagination. This provides efficient, consistent paging — even when new items are added between requests.
- [Send instream metadata for a channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/api/send-channel-instream-metadata.md): Send instream metadata for a channel

- [Architecture](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/architecture.md): How channels, ingests, engines and distributions fit together.
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/changelog.md): Find out what's new.
- [Channel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/channel.md): A channel is the core building block of the platform — it represents a complete live streaming pipeline from media ingestion through to viewer delivery. Each channel is composed of ingests, engines, and distributions. This section covers settings configured at the channel level. See the Architecture page for the full breakdown.
- [DVR window](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/channel/dvr.md): Enable time-shifted playback so viewers can rewind and catch up on live content.
- [Metadata insertion](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/channel/metadata-insertion.md): Deliver timed metadata to viewers by pushing payloads via the API or by embedding SEI messages in your source stream.
- [Nielsen audience tracking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/channel/nielsen.md): Track real-time audience measurement with Nielsen markers.
- [Ingest](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution.md)
- [Closed captions](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/closed-captions.md): How to ingest and deliver closed captions via SRT.
- [Ingest protocols](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/ingest-protocols.md): Supported ingest protocols (RTMP and SRT) and their trade-offs.
- [Multi-audio](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/multi-audio-support.md): How to ingest and deliver multiple audio tracks via SRT.
- [Using FFmpeg with OptiView Live](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/software-encoders/ffmpeg.md): Free open-source command-line tool for streaming media files.
- [Using OBS with OptiView Live](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/software-encoders/obs.md): Free open-source application for broadcasting and recording streams.
- [Using vMix with OptiView Live](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/software-encoders/vmix.md): Windows desktop application that provides a vision mixer and encoder.
- [Using Wirecast with OptiView Live](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/contribution/software-encoders/wirecast.md): Live video streaming production tool by Telestream.
- [Distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution.md)
- [Distribution override](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/distribution-override.md): Redirect viewers to a different distribution based on their platform.
- [Max bitrate](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/max-bitrate.md): Limit the output qualities on a distribution by setting a maximum bitrate.
- [Multi-distribution](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/multi-distribution.md): Serve streams through multiple distributions, each connecting to one or more engines.
- [Redundancy](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/redundancy.md): Configure failover and redundancy for high-availability streaming.
- [Security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/security.md)
- [Geo-blocking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/security/geo-blocking.md): Restrict or allow access to your streams based on viewer location.
- [IP-blocking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/security/ip-blocking.md): Block or allow access based on IP addresses.
- [Referrer blocking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/security/referrer-blocking.md): Restrict access based on the referring website or domain.
- [Token-based security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/security/token-based-security.md): Require JWT tokens for viewer authentication.
- [Thumbnails](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/thumbnails-manifests.md): Access live thumbnail images for your streams.
- [WebRTC](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/distribution/webrtc.md): Add a WebRTC source to a distribution for sub-second real-time delivery powered by OptiView Real-time Streaming.
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/getting-started.md): Create your first stream, from setup to embedding the player.
- [Hardware Encoders](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/hardware-encoders.md): Hardware-based Encoders for Broadcasting Live Streaming Content
- [Haivision](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/hardware-encoders/haivision.md): Haivision provides professional broadcasting equipment to the streaming industry. The KB series of H.264 & HEVC Internet Media Encoders and Transcoders deliver high-quality video streaming for resolutions up to 4K, available as small form factor portable appliances as well as rack-mountable enterprise-grade servers. The KB Series provides you with multiple options for live event streaming, helping you deliver the highest quality live video to your global internet audience.
- [Magewell](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/hardware-encoders/magewell.md): Magewell is a leading encoder manufacturer that supports connecting to the Dolby OptiView Streaming platform for broadcasting live streams. This guide outlines how to broadcast streams from a Magewell device to the Dolby OptiView platform.
- [Osprey](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/hardware-encoders/osprey.md): Osprey Video is a world leader in broadcasting solutions and an early adopter of WHIP for their Osprey Talon 4K-SC Encoder.
- [Videon](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/hardware-encoders/videon.md): Videon is a leading video streaming company offering innovative solutions to enhance content delivery and quality. Their flagship product, EdgeCaster, optimizes video streaming with low-latency and high-quality playback across platforms, empowering service providers to deliver seamless and engaging viewing experiences.
- [Manage organization](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/manage-team.md): Manage your organization, invite team members and assign roles.
- [Media Engine](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine.md)
- [ABR](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine/abr.md): ABR ladder configuration with resolution and bitrate rungs.
- [DRM](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine/drm.md): Protect your live streams with Digital Rights Management.
- [Encoding quality](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine/encoding-quality.md): Configure encoding quality settings for your media engine.
- [Image overlays](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine/image-overlays.md): Add static image overlays like logos or watermarks to your live stream.
- [Streaming protocols](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/media-engine/streaming-protocols.md): Output protocols supported by the platform (HESP, HLS, and WebRTC).
- [Platform](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform.md)
- [Live Clipping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/live-clipping.md): Live clipping allows you to create CDN servable media files from your HLS stream while it is in progress. This requires a Real-time streaming account and API key in addition to your Live channel. For more details on the capabilities of the Media Asset API, refer to our Media Asset API guide and contact your Dolby Account Team to help you get set up.
- [Webhooks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/real-time-update-with-webhooks.md): Receive real-time notifications when channel events occur.
- [How to Use Schedulers](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/scheduler.md): Automatically start and stop channels on a schedule.
- [Usage monitoring](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/usage.md): Track viewing minutes, bytes transferred, and transcoding minutes per channel.
- [Viewer insights](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/viewer-insights.md): Monitor concurrent viewers, latency, locations and platforms in real time.
- [Viewer tracking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/platform/viewer-tracking.md): Track viewers and syndication partners using tracking IDs and custom viewer data.
- [OptiView Player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/playback/optiview-player.md): The Dolby OptiView Streaming platform uses the OptiView Player for playback. The player is available on a wide range of platforms and supports HESP and HLS streaming, along with features like UI controls, ad insertion, and advanced APIs.
- [Other HLS player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/playback/other-hls-player.md): Play streams directly via HLS on devices like Chromecast or set-top boxes.
- [Software Encoders](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/software-encoders.md): Software-based Encoders for Broadcasting Live Streaming Content
- [Troubleshooting](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theolive/troubleshooting.md): Common issues and debugging tips.
- [OptiView Player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer.md): <DocCardList
- [OptiView Player Android SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/android.md)
- [Player API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/api-reference/roku.md): Discover all properties and functions of THEOplayer.
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/changelog.md): Find out what's new in the OptiView Player.
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/changelog/react-native.md): Find out what's new in the OptiView Player.
- [OptiView Player Chromecast SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/chromecast.md)

- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/connectors/android/uplynk/introduction.md): THEOplayer and Uplynk are partners in the streaming industry. The Uplynk Platform offers services like encoding, transcoding, server-side ad-insertion, a CDN and more.
- [Ping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/connectors/android/uplynk/ping.md): This article explains how to use the Uplynk Ping API.
- [Preplay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/connectors/android/uplynk/preplay.md): This article explains how to leverage the Uplynk Preplay API through THEOplayer. This API allows developers to pre-integrate with Uplynk Preplay service.

- [Examples](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/examples.md): See the player in action!
- [Basic player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/examples/basic.md): Code
- [Frequently asked questions (FAQ)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq.md): In this section we will list all the frequently asked questions we received over the years.
- [Can clipping be used on a playlist](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/can-clipping-be-used-in-a-playlist.md): Yes. This is in principle not different from using it on a single video: a startTime and an endTime must be given for each video and applied when the video is loaded/played.
- [Can timeline thumbnails be made available before playback start](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/can-timeline-thumbnails-be-made-available-before-playback-start.md): Yes, this is possible using the preload feature, provided your timeline is already visible. Both “metadata” and “auto” will be effective values to this end.
- [Can we show a custom message on 403 on mp4](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/can-we-show-custom-message-on-403-on-mp4.md): Yes, this is possible, but not as easy as for DASH and HLS. To address this specific use case no internal THEOplayer tool exists yet (2019) and a separate XHR call needs to be done to determine the exact status of the request. Custom texts, overlays and buttons can be showed on the player following the instructions from the related linked resource.
- [Can we use HLS or DASH ads](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/can-we-use-hls-or-dash-ads.md): Yes, we support HLS and DASH ads through a THEOAdDescription for the THEOplayer Web SDK 2.84.0 and up. HLS and DASH ads are currently not supported on any other SDKs. Our Google IMA integration does not support HLS or DASH ads.
- [How to use the CDN fallback/backup stream feature](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/cdn-fallback-backup-stream-feature.md): THEOplayer offers a feature to detect more sources of content in manifest or playlist, so that the player can automatically switch to a fallback URL, in case any of the main content source is unavailable or not reachable. This feature works for both MPEG-DASH and HLS streams out of the box, and does not require any additional configuration to the player.
- [Chromecast on my webplayer does not work any longer despite no change in my implementation](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/chromecast-on-weblayer-does-not-longer-work.md): This or a similar question may be asked in case your Chromecast stops working. There may indeed be different causes why your Chromecast is not working, especially if
- [Does THEOplayer support EXT-X-DATERANGE](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/does-theoplayer-support-ext-x-daterange.md): Yes, the HLS tag #EXT-X-DATERANGE is supported in THEOplayer as of 2.61.0. To enable it, hlsDateRange needs to be added to either the player configuration or the source description and be set to true. The date range metadata will be made available through the TextTrack API.
- [What does the error message “Something went wrong determining the initial period of the provided MPEG-DASH stream” mean](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/error-message-initial-period-provided-MPEG-DASH.md): This error, "Something went wrong determining the initial period of the provided MPEG-DASH stream", occurs whenever the player struggles to play a DASH stream due to problems determining the availability of the segments for that stream. The recommended solution for this is to make sure that the segments result available, based on the data provided in the manifest, at the time at which the manifest is requested.
- [How can I distribute 4K content?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-can-i-distribute-4k-content.md): The ability to play 4k mostly depends on hardware.
- [How does Media Engagement Index (MEI) affect Autoplay on Chrome](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-does-mei-affect-autoplay-on-chrome.md): The question above is related to the following questions:
- [How to apply accurate buffering strategy](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-apply-accurate-buffering-strategy.md): By default, THEOplayer buffers 20 seconds of video including audio/subtitles in order to guarantee smooth playout.
- [How can we avoid that the player keeps looking for chunks/segments if they are not found](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-avoid-loading-chuncks-when-not-found.md): You can use the Network API events to detect whether the stream is offline and implement the behavior you prefer. This is working for HLS and is being implemented (2020) for DASH as well.
- [How to change text in THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-change-text-in-theoplayer.md): Using language localization, you should be able to change any text in the player to the one you desire.
- [Change text when Airplaying](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-change-text-when-airplaying.md): You will need to add the 'metadata' parameter to the SourceDescription with the 'title' property filled with the title to be displayed.
- [How to combat autoplay policies](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-combat-autoplay-policies.md): This short article explains what autoplay is, where autoplay is restricted, and what THEOplayer does to facilitate working with autoplay.
- [How to create a (great) ticket](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-create-a-ticket.md): Customers can create tickets through our Jira Service Desk at https://servicedesk.theoplayer.com.
- [How to detect if the user changes the video quality](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-detect-if-user-changes-video-quality.md): It is possible to distinguish between when the quality is changed by ABR or by the user and, in the latter case, which quality has been selected.
- [How to investigate a ticket](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-investigate-a-ticket.md): This article describes how our team typically investigates an issue (i.e. bug) reported to us through a Service Desk ticket.
- [How to know whether a live stream is playing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-know-when-livestream-is-playing.md): You can check if a stream being played is live or VOD by checking its duration. If the duration returns Infinity, the stream would be live. Examples:
- [What PiP options to use to make the video stay visible while browsing or on other applications](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-make-pip-video-stay-visible-while-browsing.md): When it comes to Picture-in-Picture (PiP), there are different options, each with its advantages and disadvantages. To have the floating video remain visible during browsing, you need an out-of-app PiP, but not all features are available (or the same) everywhere.
- [How to navigate through the documentation and resources](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-navigate-through-the-documentation-and-resources.md): THEOplayer offers documentation and resources through a number of channels.
- [How to prevent screen recording](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-prevent-screen-recording.md): This article explains what screen recording is, and how to counter it.
- [How to remove CORS restrictions from a reproduction stream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-remove-cors-restrictions.md): A "CORS-issue" is a common cause of a stream not starting. In a nutshell, a CORS issue implies that the origin is not allowed to fetch the resource. This issue has to be resolved from the server-side.
- [How to remove unwanted CC track in iOS or Safari](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-remove-unwanted-cc-track-ios-safari.md): This article is intended to show how to remove any unwanted CC tracks that might be showing up in iOS or Safari. This can be done by editing the HLS master playlist.
- [How to track network errors](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-track-network-errors.md): THEOplayer currently doesn't throw an error event for all network request errors on all platforms. Sometimes you might want an event to listen for to track network related issues in your QoS/QoE dashboards. To enable this, you can use our Network API.
- [How to use the Media Session API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-use-media-session-api.md): Developers who implement the Media Session API give viewers the option to control and monitor media playback outside their web page.
- [How to use ProGuard with THEOplayer Android SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-use-proguard-with-android-sdk.md): ProGuard is a popular tool to obfuscate and optimize Java code in Android projects. Depending on the settings, this tool can clash with the THEOplayer Android SDK.
- [How to use THEOplayer iOS SDK on an M1 mac](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/how-to-use-theoplayer-ios-sdk-on-m1.md): The iPhone simulators on M1 MacBooks use the arm64 architecture, therefore any binaries linked during compilation time that do not support the architecture will result in compilation error. Many third party frameworks lack the support for this particular architecture, and this causes unfortunate limitations for development.
- [Is it a problem if the viewer pauses a live stream for longer than the DVR window](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/is-it-a-problem-if-viewer-pauses-live-stream-longer-than-dvr-window.md): In general, this is no problem as the player will continue playback, once exhausted the current buffer, from the nearest point available, in this case the end of the sliding window.
- [Is it possible to see 360 degrees photo with THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/is-it-possible-to-see-360-picture.md): This question is asked by those users and developers that are wondering if the player 360 degree functionality can be used to view also images.
- [Is Portrait mode supported](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/is-portrait-mode-supported.md): Yes. THEOplayer supports vertical video playback, which is also known as portrait mode.
- [Is YouTube supported](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/is-youtube-supported.md): This question above is typically asked by customers who have experience with the YouTube embedded player. It's also related to the following questions:
- [ITP2.1 problems using THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/itp21-problems-using-theoplayer.md): Apple’s Intelligent Tracking Prevention 2.1 causes cookies to be only saved for 7 days unless the Safari user directly interacts with the website again.
- [MediaTek limitations](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/mediatek-limitations.md): This page overviews a list of known issues caused by the MediaTek chipset.
- [Widevine CDM deprecation notice for old browser versions](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/old-widevine-cdm-deprecation-notice.md): Google announced that the current version of the Widevine Content Decryption Module (CDM), which is a closed environment responsible for handling the DRM restrictions and decryption of media, will stop working on 6 December 2022.
- [Page and Source domains](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/page-and-source-domains.md): Page and Source domains prevent unauthorized users from embedding your SDK on their website. We highly recommend configuring both Page and Source domains carefully to protect your THEOplayer license.
- [Is it possible to have multiple player instances play at the same time](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/possible-to-have-multiple-player-instances-at-same-time.md): Yes, this is possible, provided there are enough bandwidth and RAM for this to work (considering both server and client side). Although we don’t explicitly advise against this, we see how this could not be an ideal solution for the viewers. Please also keep in mind that this incurs into some limitations.
- [Is it possible to preload VOD content while the pre-roll is playing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/preload-vod-content-when-pre-roll-is-playing.md): Yes, it is done with the preload feature. Before setting your source, you need to activate preload on your player. We advise to use the value ‘auto’: this allows the first segments of the stream to be downloaded during the ad, reducing the start-up time of the video content.
- [Can we prevent UpNext feature from redirecting](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/prevent-up-next-from-redirecting.md): Yes, this is possible. You need to modify the countdownDuration value in your UpNextPanel to Infinity.
- [Removing context menu 'Powered by THEOplayer...'](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/removing-context-menu-powered-by.md): The context menu can be removed while building your player by disabling the "contextmenu" feature or by adding a CSS rule on your page.
- [Self-hosting and versioning of THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/self-hosting-theoplayer.md): Your licensed THEOplayer can also be hosted on your own servers or CDN.
- [List of supported platforms](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/supported-platforms.md): List of supported platforms for the different THEOplayer SDKs
- [The provided video source is incompatible with the license for this player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/the-provided-video-source-is-incompatible-with-the-license-for-this-player.md): The player throws this error when the source of the player is incompatible with the license. This occurs when the domain of the source is not in the list of source domains of the license. For example, when you have yourfirstcdn.com and yoursecondcdn.com as source domains, but you use a video stream (i.e. source) from https://yourthirdcdn.com/video.m3u8, then the player will throw the above error because yourthirdcdn.com is not included in the source domains (yourfirstcdn.com and yoursecondcdn.com).
- [THEOplayer Features & Modules](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/theoplayer-features-modules.md): The THEOplayer SDK consists of modular features.
- [What are the benefits of preloading](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-are-the-benefits-of-preloading.md): Choosing whether to preload parts of the video before it starts playing will help you considerably improve the user experience.
- [What are the limitations of AirPlay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-are-the-limitations-of-AirPlay.md): When casting through AirPlay your viewer is relying on the native Apple video player.
- [What are the player seeking and seeked events and when are they fired](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-are-the-player-seeking-and-seeked-events.md): They are events related to the playback of media files. seeking is fired when the user starts seeking/moving to a new position. seeked is fired when the user is finished seeking/moving to a new position and the player has buffered a video portion, being therefore ready to play.
- [What are the Uplynk challenges](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-are-the-uplynk-challenges.md): The purpose of this article is to group common challenges application developers might encounter when using Uplynk (formerly known as Verizon Media, Edgecast or Edgio) as the back-end.
- [What does the error message 'can only be initiated by a user gesture' imply? Can I still force the desired action](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-does-error-can-only-be-initiated-by-user-gesture-mean.md): You may get this error if you are trying to have the player execute an action without it being initiated by the user. A typical example is going fullscreen or automatically starting playback (often on mobile).
- [What does the error message 'Something went wrong with Native playback' mean](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-does-error-something-went-wrong-during-playback-mean.md): This error, "Something went wrong with Native playback", occurs whenever the player struggles on the native rendering pipeline of the browser in use. The error code itself can be different, and it reflects the HTML5 MediaError interface.
- [What does the error message 'Unknown CDM error' mean](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-does-error-unknown-cdm-mean.md): If you got to see this page, you probably know that CDM stands for Content Decryption Module, which is a component in the DRM pipeline, a part of the content protection process.
- [What is an impression](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-is-an-impression.md): This article explains what an impression is and how THEOplayer calculates impressions.
- [What is the collaboration between Azure Media Services and THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-is-the-collaboration-between-azure-media-services-and-THEOplayer.md): Azure Media Services has been retired since June 30th, 2024.
- [What is the support for Wowza](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-is-the-support-for-wowza.md): Wowza and THEOplayer are partners in the streaming industry. When developers use both products,
- [What is the "WebAssembly.compileStreaming failed" warning and what does it mean?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/what-is-the-webAssembly-compileStreaming-failed-warning.md): When starting playback in THEOplayer, you might be seeing a warning in console that reads
- [What aspects of THEOplayer do we need to take into account to deploy a proper Content Security Policy (CSP)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/whats-aspects-to-take-into-account-when-developing-csp.md): The script-src 'self' and 'inline' should be allowed. The player also requires doing calls to \*.theoplayer.com and to wherever the JavaScript files and workers are located. Additionally, depending on your active features, you may need to add some other source (e.g.//www.gstatic.com/, as its library is hosted there).
- [Which error related events does the player expose](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/which-error-related-events-does-player-expose.md): THEOplayer exposes different types of errors. Refer to "How to do error handling"
- [Which network calls (or requests) does THEOplayer do](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/which-network-calls-does-THEOplayer-do.md): The question above is related to the following questions:
- [Which source is the one being played](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/which-source-is-the-one-being-played.md): In THEOplayer, player.source.sources can be used to provide alternative sources for playback, for cross-platform support. What is the actual source being played, if more than one is provided that is supported on the current platform/browser?
- [Which subtitle and CC formats are supported on native Safari](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/which-subtitle-and-cc-formats-are-supported-on-native-safari.md): The Safari browser on iOS uses the native Safari video player.
- [Why are not all response headers exposed](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-are-not-all-response-headers-exposed.md): You may ask this question if, for example, you are trying to intercept a certain XHR response to use data contained in custom headers but you don't find these among the exposed headers (see documentation below regarding network interceptors).
- [Why can't I select another video quality on iOS/tvOS?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-cant-we-select-other-video-quality-on-ios-tvos.md): In short: Apple technically restricts video players from doing this, and there is no workaround.
- [Why did my subtitles stop working](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-did-subtitles-stop-working.md): This question is occasionally asked by developers who've configured the latest release of THEOplayer.
- [Why does Chromecast not work on iOS Chrome?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-chromecast-not-work-on-ios-chrome.md): iOS Chrome used to support Chromecast. However, Google silently removed support for it at some point. It is also documented on their Web Sender guide here that:
- [Why does the currentTime seem off in my livestream & what can I do about it](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-currentime-seem-to-be-off.md): THEOplayer has a currentTime property, which returns you the current playback position in the video (in seconds). For VOD this works as you would expect. If you have a video, let it play for 30 seconds and query the currentTime, the player will report 30 seconds. However, if you start watching a livestream and query the currentTime after 30 seconds, you won't get 30 seconds reported. Nor will you get the time that would have elapsed if you watched the segments in the most recent manifest file until the point where you queried the currentTime. This might seem odd, but there's a very good reason for this, which we'll explain in a bit, along with some workarounds if you do want to use one of the aforementioned definitions of currentTime in a live streaming context.
- [Why does fullscreen not behave as expected on iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-fullscreen-not-behave-as-expected-on-ios.md): One of the most frequently encountered issues on iOS devices is unexpected behavior concerning fullscreen playback.
- [Why does the Network API not work on iOS devices](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-network-api-not-work-on-ios-devices.md): Most Network API capabilities are not available on iOS devices. Generally speaking, video playback on iOS behaves like a black box. This black box handles the requests (and responses) related to video manifests and segments, which makes them impossible to intercept. This limits the possible uses of the Network API on such devices.
- [Why does the playback not work when using the Chrome iPhone/iPad simulator](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-playback-not-work-using-chrome-iphone-simulator.md): You might need an answer to this question if you are testing your page using the Chrome iPhone/iPad simulator and notice that, reloading the page, the video player stops working (even though the ads, if any, are displayed correctly) and some errors are displayed in the console.
- [Why does the player load only one audio track (even though there are several in the manifest)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-does-player-load-only-one-audio-track.md): You may be asking this question if you notice a discrepancy in the number of audio tracks present in the manifest and the audio tracks that you can select from the player UI.
- [Why are the FairPlay license and certificate being requested again on iOS after ads?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-is-DRM-license-retrieved-again-after-ads-on-iOS.md): The DRM license and certificate are retrieved when a DRM-protected stream starts to be played. This is usually enough for a full playback session since in most cases the DRM context can be maintained.
- [Why is my PlayReady stream not working in Chromium Edge?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/Why-is-my-Playready-stream-not-working-in-Chromium-Edge.md): If you are having problems playing PlayReady streams in Chromium Edge, this may be due to a conflict between player and browser, regarding the DRM system to use. If this is the case, you may solve the issue indicating Playready as your first option with the preferredKeySystems property.
- [Why is my video not playing automatically](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-is-video-not-playing-automatically.md): There is a wide variety of possible causes that bring to a video not being automatically played. These may have to do with autoplay policies, iframes, Media Engagement Index and more. Please read the full answer in this article for more information. Also, please keep in mind that this is not an extensive list of causes for autoplay failure and that, as time goes by, browsers autoplay management and autoplay policies may change.
- [Why the Visibility API does not work through an iframe on Safari and IE11](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/faq/why-visibilty-api-doesnt-work-in-iframe-on-safari-and-ie11.md): This question may be asked when you observe a different behavior of the Visibility API on different browsers.
- [OptiView Player Flutter SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter.md)
- [Changelog](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/changelog.md): Find out what's new in the OptiView Player.

- [Creating a minimal demo app](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/creating-minimal-app.md): In this section we start from an empty Flutter project, include a dependency to fluttertheoplayersdk, and deploy it on an Android or iOS device.
- [Using a custom DRM integration](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/custom_drm.md): The initial version of the THEOplayer Flutter SDK only support generic Widevine and Fairplay DRM playback.
- [Custom branches](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/custom-branches.md): THEOplayer Flutter SDK is built on top of the existing THEOplayer iOS, Android, and Web SDKs. However, not all native SDK features are available in Flutter from the start.
- [Debug Flags (Experimental)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/debug-flags.md): Overview

- [Picture-in-Picture support](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/picture-in-picture.md): THEOplayer Flutter SDK supports native Picture-in-Picture on iOS, Android and Web platforms.
- [THEOlive support](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/theolive.md): High-quality synchronized video streaming at scale for providers of large sports broadcasting, sports betting, and interactive entertainment
- [The THEOplayer component](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/theoplayer-component.md): Architecture
- [WASM support (experimental)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/flutter/guides/wasm_support.md): When compiling the THEOplayer Flutter SDK for WEB, you can now specify WASM support.

- [Migrating to THEOplayer Android SDK 11.x](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/android/migrating-to-theoplayer-11.md): This article will guide you through updating to THEOplayer Android SDK version 11 (from version 10),

- [Getting started on Chromecast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/chromecast/getting-started.md): This how-to guide describes how to set up a Chromecast Receiver application using the Dolby OptiView Player SDK.
- [How to update an SDK?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/how-to-update-a-sdk.md): This how-to guide describes how to update to a new version of the THEOplayer SDK.
- [SDKs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/introduction.md): Dolby OptiView Player provides a set of feature-rich video player software development kits (SDKs).
- [THEOplayer iOS/tvOS 5.0 Feature Integrations](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/ios/features.md): Modularization
- [Getting started on iOS/tvOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/ios/getting-started.md): Introduction
- [Migrating to THEOplayer iOS/tvOS SDK 11.x](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/ios/migrating-to-theoplayer-11.md): This article will guide you through updating to THEOplayer iOS/tvOS SDK version 11 (from version 10),
- [Playing ads with THEOplayer Roku SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/roku/displaying-ads.md): This article will provide the steps needed to play video ads with your content using the THEOplayer Roku SDK. The THEOplayer Roku SDK currently only supports Client-side Ad Insertion (CSAI) for VOD assets and some Server-side Ad Insertion (SSAI) using Google Ad Manager or THEOads. It does not yet support Client-side Ad Stitching (CSAS), Server Guided Ad Insertion (SGAI) or live CSAI ads.
- [Getting Started on Roku](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/roku/getting-started.md): This getting started guide gets you familiar with Roku and the Dolby OptiView Player Roku SDK.
- [Migrating to THEOplayer Roku SDK 11.x](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/roku/migrating-to-theoplayer-11.md): This article will guide you through updating to THEOplayer Roku SDK version 11 (from version 10.x),
- [Setting up a Tizen device for debugging](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/tizen/generating-a-certificate.md): Tizen requires a certificate to launch an application on a (physical) device. Below are the steps on how to create one:
- [Getting started on Tizen](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/tizen/getting-started.md): This how-to guide describes how to set up a Tizen application using the Dolby OptiView Player SDK on Tizen.
- [Installing the Tizen developer tools](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/tizen/installing-tizen-studio.md): - Check if your device meets the Tizen developer tools prerequisites.
- [Deploying a test app on a Tizen emulator](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/tizen/setting-up-emulator.md): We strongly recommend using a physical Tizen device. If this is not possible, using an emulator can be a back-up.
- [Deploying a test app on a physical Tizen device](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/tizen/setting-up-physical.md): Both the physical Tizen device as the development computer must be on the same network.
- [API examples](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/api-examples.md): This page contains an overview of examples, demonstrating the capabilities of the THEOplayer API and custom JavaScript. With these examples, we try to explain the fundamentals of how to use our API:
- [Getting started on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/getting-started.md): Dolby OptiView Player is the universal video player solution from Dolby.
- [Getting started with Accessibility](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/getting-started-accessibility.md): Integrating accessibility features on the web is crucial to accommodating viewers with limitations, optimize the general viewer experience and maximize overall reach. The basis of video accessibility means that viewers with disabilities can perceive, understand, navigate, and engage with video content equally, and without obstacles or barriers. Additionally, accessibility can apply to all viewers, regardless of disability or not (i.e. situational limitations, slow internet connection, temporary disabilities, etc.). In this guide we discuss what guidelines to follow to make your content accessible, the legal bodies in place, why accessibility is important, as well as what features are available for OptiView player to make video content accessible.
- [How to embed an iframe](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-can-we-embed-iframe.md): Embedding a video player through an `` can be interesting for a number of reasons:
- [How to customize quality selection and labels on the default UI (MP4)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-to-customize-quality-selection.md): Some of our customers prefer MP4 assets over HLS/MPEG-DASH ones. One major downside to this approach is that Adaptive Bitrate Streaming (ABR) is not possible with such streams, as they don't have a playlist with multiple qualities.
- [How to get frame-accurate currentTime display in the UI Control bar](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-to-get-frame-accurate-currentime-display.md): This article will show you how to display a frame-accurate currentTime on your THEOplayer instance UI control bar. For more information regarding the currentTime itself, please refer to the reference API.
- [How to implement Keyboard Hotkeys](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-to-implement-hotkeys.md): This how-to guide describes how you can implement keyboard shortcuts in THEOplayer.
- [How to implement a seamless transition between videos?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-to-implement-seamless-transition.md): You may need an answer to this question if you want to play one video after the other without letting the user know the source has changed (to the user they will seem as if playing just one video). Similar questions may be:

- [How to work around browser cache with new THEOplayer SDK libraries?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/how-to-work-around-browser-cache-with-new-license.md): In some cases you may experience problems related to cached versions of the THEOplayer library (e.g. if you are dealing with users who are getting expired license errors even though you already deployed new SDK libraries).
- [Migrating to THEOplayer Web SDK 11.x](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/getting-started/sdks/web/migrating-to-theoplayer-11.md): This article will guide you through updating to THEOplayer Web SDK version 11 (from version 10),

- [How-to guides](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides.md): Want to start implementing features to your SDK? Our how-to-guides will help you with all kind of integrations!
- [Adblock detection](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/block-detection.md): The THEOplayer Ads API allows developers to detect adblockers, and react accordingly.
- [Custom Server Side Ad Insertion](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/custom-ssai-integration.md): THEOplayer provides a way to integrate with third-party advertisement providers,
- [Customizing the ad overlay text](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/customizing-the-ad-overlay-text.md): This guide explains how to customize the overlay text shown during an ad break. You may ask this question if you want to change the customize the styling or override the texts shown during an ad break.
- [FreeWheel](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/freewheel.md): This guide explains how to set up THEOplayer in combination with FreeWheel, an advertisement vendor.
- [Google Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/google-dai.md): Google DAI is a Server-Side Ad-Insertion solution offered by Google where THEOplayer is pre-integrated and offers playback for HLS and DASH Streams. A demo can be found at https://demo.theoplayer.com/google-dai.
- [Google IMA](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/google-ima.md): THEOplayer offer supports for Google IMA as an ad integration system.
- [How to display visual cues on the timeline to mark mid-rolls?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/how-to-display-visual-cues.md): You may ask this question if you want to let the user know when and how many mid-rolls there are during the video.
- [How to set up VAST and VMAP ads?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/how-to-set-up-vast-and-vmap.md): This article explains how to schedule client-side VAST and VMAP advertisements through THEOplayer.
- [How to subscribe to ad events](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/how-to-subscribe-to-ad-events.md): When building a custom UI, or when logging events to an analytics service, app developers often need to be able to listen (and react) to ad events. For example, if an ad break starts, they want to draw a countdown on top of their UI. Alternatively, when an ad error occurs, they want to log this to an analytics server.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/introduction.md): The articles in this section describe how to implement common use-cases related to advertisements. These use-cases can range from customizing the UX during advertisements, to detecting ad blockers.
- [MediaTailor](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/mediatailor.md): AWS Elemental MediaTailor is a service that provides scalable ad insertion and channel assembly. It is able to serve targeted ad content to viewers and create linear streams while maintaining broadcast quality in over-the-top (OTT) video applications. It supports HLS and DASH for both VOD and live workflows.
- [Prebid.js](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/prebidjs.md): This article explains how you can connect THEOplayer with Prebid.js. Prebid.js is an open-source header bidding solution. On the client-side, you can request bids from multiple ad networks, take a winning bid and select its associated advertisement. You want to configure and play this selected advertisement in THEOplayer.
- [SpotX](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/spotx.md): SpotX is a global video ad serving platform providing digital media owners with a solution that allows them to monetize their content with video advertising across desktop, mobile and connected devices.
- [Yospace](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ads/yospace.md): Yospace is a Server-Side Ad Insertion solution. A demo for the Web SDK can be found here.
- [Adobe Analytics](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/adobe-analytics.md): Adobe Streaming Media Collection Add-on is a video analytics service, and is part of Adobe Experience Cloud.
- [Agama](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/agama.md): Agama is an analytics solution maintained by Agama Technologies. THEOplayer offers a pre-integration for this service.
- [Conviva Connector for VideoAnalytics & AdsAnalytics](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/conviva/conviva-connector.md): This article describes on how to integrate Conviva for Video Analytics and Ads Analytics offered as a connector with THEOplayer SDK.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/conviva/introduction.md): Conviva is an analytics service. THEOplayer offers a connector which can be initialized depending upon the
- [Custom Analytics Integration](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/custom-analytics-integration.md): As mentioned in our Introduction, video analytics provide tremendous insights into your user's QoE, bring extra business intelligence and enhance decision-making.

- [MediaMelon SmartSight](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/mediamelon-smartsight.md): MediaMelon SmartSight is an analytics service maintained by MediaMelon. The library is maintained by the MediaMelon team.
- [Moat](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/moat.md): Moat is an analytics service maintained by Oracle. THEOplayer offers a pre-integration for this solution. A demo can be found at https://demo.theoplayer.com/moat-analytics-pre-integration.
- [Mux](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/mux.md): Mux /data is a video analytics service. Mux is currently the maintainer of the Mux-THEOplayer pre-integration.
- [Segment](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/segment.md): Segment is a Customer Data Platform, and offers integrations with
- [Tealium](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/tealium.md): Tealium is a tag management system and offers integrations with Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, and others.
- [Wicket Labs](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/wicket-labs.md): Wicket Labs is an audience insights platform for video, and offers products like the "Wicket Scoreboard".
- [Youbora (NPAW Plugin)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/analytics/youbora.md): This article is a good place to start if you are looking for information on how to configure THEOplayer with Youbora.
- [Getting started with CMCD on Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/android/cmcd/getting-started.md): Request Mode
- [Media3 pipeline for Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/android/media3/getting-started.md): The THEOplayer Android Media3 pipeline is a new and rebuilt base layer for playback of video and audio files for the THEOplayer Android SDK.
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/android/theolive/getting-started.md): To begin with the Dolby OptiView platform please review our guide for getting started with OptiView Streaming.
- [Token based security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/android/theolive/token-based-security.md): OptiView Live offers the option to enable JWT token security on a distribution (formerly referred to as an alias) level. This can be interesting if you only want valid users to access your stream. Read more about the feature and configuring it on your channels on the token based security guide.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/airplay/introduction.md): AirPlay is a casting technology developed by Apple.
- [Connecting from custom Sender applications](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/connecting-from-custom-sender-applications.md): General
- [Debugging](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/debugging.md): This how-to guide describes how to debug a Chromecast Receiver application using the THEOplayer Web SDK.
- [Enable Chromecast on the Sender](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/enable-chromecast-on-the-sender.md): This article explains how to enable the Chromecast Sender capabilities on the Web SDK, iOS SDK and Android SDK.
- [How to configure a different stream to Chromecast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/how-to-configure-to-a-different-stream.md): Some streaming setups require you to cast a different stream to a Chromecast Receiver device than the one playing on a Chromecast Receiver device.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/introduction.md): In this section you will learn everything about how THEOplayer works with Chromecast.
- [Pass subtitle selection on to Chromecast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/pass-subtitle-section-on-to-chromecast.md): Currently, it is intended behavior that when you start casting the player to a Chromecast device the subtitle selection setting isn't carried over. A new player is generated with the SourceConfiguration of the sender, but not any adjustments.
- [Sending messages from/to Sender to/from Receiver](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/cast/chromecast/sending-messages-from-to-sender-to-from-receiver.md): This article will explain how to send data to an active receiver and how to respond on the receiver.
- [Arris Titanium](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/arris-titanium.md): This article describes how to configure Arris Titanium DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Axinom](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/axinom.md): This article describes how to configure Axinom DRM with THEOplayer.
- [How to use THEOplayer with KeyOS BuyDRM](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/buydrm-keyos/how-to-use-with-theoplayer.md): This guide explains how you set up THEOplayer in combination with the KeyOS BuyDRM, a partnered multi-DRM vendor. THEOplayer is pre-integrated with KeyOS and provides a user-friendly API to connect to their Widevine and PlayReady DRM solutions.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/buydrm-keyos/introduction.md): This article describes how to configure BuyDRM KeyOS DRM with THEOplayer.

- [How to do offline playback with DRMToday on iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/castlabs-drmtoday/offline-playback-with-offline-drm.md): This how-to guide describes how to implement playback of downloadable content-protected HLS streams using the THEOplayer iOS SDK. Currently, FairPlay is supported together with the Irdeto and DRMToday integrations.
