- [Customizing Notifications for Downloading Offline DRM on Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/how-to-do-offline-drm/offline-drm-notifications-android.md): Text Resources Customization
- [How to do offline DRM on Android](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/how-to-do-offline-drm/offline-drm-with-android.md): This how-to guide describes how to implement playback of downloadable content-protected MPEG-DASH using the THEOplayer Android SDK. Currently, Widevine is supported together with the Irdeto and DRMToday integrations.
- [How to do offline DRM on iOS/tvOS SDK and Legacy iOS/tvOS SDK (4.12.x)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/how-to-do-offline-drm/offline-drm-with-ios.md): Prerequisites
- [How to play a Clear-Key-protected stream in THEOplayer?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/how-to-play-clear-key-protected-stream.md): This article provides a simple example of how to use Clear Key with THEOplayer.
- [Intertrust ExpressPlay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/intertrust-expressplay.md): This article describes how to configure Intertrust ExpressPlay DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/introduction.md): THEOplayer supports FairPlay, PlayReady and Widevine by default. To connect to such a DRM system, developers can use a ContentProtectionDescription (or DRMConfiguration, depending on the SDK).
- [Irdeto](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/irdeto.md): This article describes how to configure Irdeto DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Microsoft Azure](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/microsoft-azure.md): Azure Media Services has been retired since June 30th, 2024.
- [Multi-key HLS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/multikey-hls.md): There are multiple DRM key systems on the market to choose from. The most popular ones are Google Widevine, Microsoft PlayReady and Apple FairPlay. Under the hood, all these systems encrypt the media samples using the AES-128 encryption algorithm. AES-128 is a standardized block-cipher that allows for multiple ways of choosing the blocks. Widevine and PlayReady both use CTR and FairPlay uses CBC. Therefore, it was possible to share segments between Widevine and PlayReady protected streams, but not with FairPlay protected stream.
- [Nagra Conax Contego](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/nagra-conax-contego.md): This article describes how to configure Conax DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Uplynk](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/uplynk.md): Important: Uplynk customers should configure DRM through the THEOplayer Uplynk integration.
- [Verimatrix MultiRights](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/verimatrix-multirights.md): This article describes how to configure Verimatrix MultiRights with THEOplayer. MultiRights is a multi-DRM solution by Verimatrix.
- [Vimond](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/vimond.md): This article describes how to configure Vimond DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Vualto VuDRM](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/vualto-vudrm.md): This article describes how to configure Vualto VuDRM with THEOplayer.
- [Xstream](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/drm/xstream.md): This article describes how to configure Xstream DRM with THEOplayer.
- [Basic Playback Guide](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/flutter/theolive/basic-playback-guide.md): After following our Getting Started with Flutter guide or the Player Setup section below, you will be ready to programmatically perform common playback actions. These include increasing/decreasing
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/flutter/theolive/getting-started.md): Usage
- [Deliver Low-Latency HLS live streams using Wowza Streaming Engine and THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/integrations/deliver-low-latency-hls-live-streams-with-wowza-streaming-engine.md): Wowza Streaming Engine™ media server software version 4.7.8 and later together with THEOplayer version 2.65 and later can deliver Low-Latency HLS (LL-HLS) live streams.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/integrations/introduction.md): In this section, you can figure out how THEOplayer integrates with various partners.
- [IRIS.TV](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/integrations/iris-tv.md): Iris.TV is a recommendation engine. When hooking THEOplayer to the IrisTV SDK, a UI will be overlaid over the default player UI which can be used to navigate through recommendations.
- [Wowza streaming engine with secure token version 2](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/integrations/wowza-streaming-engine-with-secure-token-version-2.md): This guide explains how to set up THEOplayer with Wowza Streaming Engine™, using their SecureToken version 2 module, that provides playback protection via a security token that is exchanged between the server and clients. The SecureToken is a challenge/response system that helps to protect content against spoofing threats. Each connection is protected by a random key and a password (shared secret).
- [Getting started with CMCD on iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ios/cmcd/getting-started.md): Request Mode
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ios/theolive/getting-started.md): Usage
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/license/introduction.md): This article explains how to configure the license at runtime.
- [How to programmatically detect audio track changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-detect-audio-track-changes.md): This article describes how you can use the THEOplayer API to detect audio track quality changes.
- [How to programmatically detect audio tracks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-detect-audio-tracks.md): This article describes how you can use the API to detect audio tracks.
- [How to programmatically detect video track qualities](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-detect-video-track-qualities.md): This article describes how you can use the API to detect video track qualities.
- [How to programmatically detect video track quality changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-detect-video-track-quality-changes.md): This article describes how you can use the API to detect video track quality changes.
- [How to programmatically enable or disable audio tracks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-enable-disable-audio-tracks.md): This article describes how you can use the MediaTrack API to enable or disable audio tracks.
- [How to reduce data usage on mobile devices](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-reduce-data-usage-on-mobile-devices.md): Customers that use THEOplayer in mobile web which leverage THEOplayer's preloading capabilities (preload = "auto"), or provide very high qualities, sometimes receive complaints about data usage.
- [How to programmatically select a video track quality](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-select-video-track-quality.md): This article describes how you can use the API to select a video track quality. If you select a specific quality, you overrule the ABR algorithm.
- [How to start with a specific quality?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/how-to-start-with-specific-quality.md): This question is typically asked because developers want their HLS or MPEG-DASH stream to start with a specific rendition of the manifest. They want to implement this use-case to speed up the time-to-first frame, or to serve a better quality to the customer, or to serve a different quality on different platforms.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/mediatrack/introduction.md): This section provides some examples to help with the most common use cases:
- [Clipping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/clipping.md): THEOplayer supports playing only a selected part of a video-on-demand stream, rather than playing it from start to finish. This can be used for example to watch a single game-changing event from a sports match, highlight an important part of a long video presentation, or skip straight to the best part of a song's video clip.
- [How to change the context menu](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/context-menu/how-to-change-context-menu.md): This question is typically asked by developers who want to change the logo in the context menu, change the default text or add some items.
- [How can we disable the right click?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/context-menu/how-to-disable-right-click.md): There are 2 ways of disabling the right click menu on the player.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/context-menu/introduction.md): This section gives you the answer to the following questions:
- [Error codes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/error/error-codes.md): THEOplayer has different types of error events.
- [How to do error handling](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/error/how-to-do-error-handling.md): As a developer, you could be interested in error handling for any of the following reasons:
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/error/introduction.md): We live in an imperfect world, and sometimes things go wrong.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/introduction.md): In this section, you can find guides on how to use many of the player features not addressed in other sections of this documentation.
- [Picture-in-Picture](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/picture-in-picture.md): This guide explains what Picture-in-Picture (PiP) is, describes how to implement it and links relevant resources.
- [Preloading](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/preloading.md): This article contains a description of the possible preloading settings and will show you how to use them on your player. This is possible on Web, Android and iOS SDKs. The aim of preloading is to improve user experience by loading parts of the video before the Play button is hit when the user is likely to play the video.
- [Related Content](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/related-content.md): The article in this section describes how to implement common use-cases related to the Related Content feature. This feature adds a component to the UI which enables viewers to navigate to related content. Developers can configure the related content using the exposed Related API.
- [Saving the progress of a caching task in iOS](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/saving-cachingtask-progress-ios.md): This guide explains how to save the progress of an ongoing caching task and what the limitations of the iOS platform are.
- [Social Sharing](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/social-sharing.md): This article describes how to implement common use cases related to Social Sharing feature.
- [Up Next](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/up-next.md): This article describes how to implement common use cases related to the Up Next feature - for example how to use the API.
- [Using hlsDateRange property](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/using-hlsdatarange-property.md): This article explains how to enable the DateRange feature, so that a text track and cues are created.
- [VR](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/vr.md): This article describes how to implement common use cases related to VR / 360 - for example how to use the API.
- [Web Audio](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/miscellaneous/webaudio.md): THEOplayer provides a Web Audio API, which allows you to do some quite nifty stuff with the player's audio output. If you are unfamiliar with the ideas behind Web Audio API, Mozilla has an excellent intro to the basic ideas you need to know about here. This guide will help you set up a demo just like the one we have in our Demo Zone.
- [Android Network Request/Response Interceptor](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/network/android-network-interceptor.md): THEOplayer Android SDK makes a number of network requests and receives responses while playing video. These requests and responses can be intercepted to make a new or modified request or respond with a new response.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/network/introduction.md): This article explains how to use the Network API.
- [iOS HLS Media Playlist Interceptor](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/network/ios-hls-media-playlist-interceptor.md): THEOplayer iOS SDK makes a number of network requests and receives responses while playing video. These requests and responses can be intercepted to make a new or modified request, or respond with a new response to the HLS playlist requests.
- [Network Request/Response Interceptors](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/network/request-response-interceptors.md): THEOplayer makes a number of network requests and receives responses while playing content video, these requests and responses can be intercepted to make a new or modified request or respond with a new response. The network object thus allows easy access to handle the network efficiently.
- [How can we track the first play(ing) event?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/player/how-can-we-track-the-first-playing-event.md): The question above is commonly asked to assist with the use-cases where the first play event thrown by THEOplayer for a specific stream needs to be detected. Similar questions are:
- [How do you know when a stream or an advertisement is playing?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/player/how-do-you-know-when-a-stream-or-ad-is-playing.md): This short how-to guide explains how to determine programmatically when the player is playing and whether it is playing a stream or a client-side advertisement. This may be useful if you need to determine different behavior (or UI) relating to the 2 mentioned options.
- [How to measure time-to-first-frame?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/player/how-to-measure-time-to-first-frame.md): The question above is related to the following questions:
- [How to track player states](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/player/how-to-track-player-states.md): A video player has states. As a developer, you should understand these states,
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/player/introduction.md): The Player API is the top-level API. This API exposes:
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/react-native/theolive/getting-started.md): Usage
- [Token based security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/react-native/theolive/token-based-security.md): OptiView Live offers the option to enable JWT token security on channel distribution level. This can be interesting if you only want valid users to access your stream. Read more about the feature and configuring it on your channels on the token based security guide.
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/roku/theolive/getting-started.md): Usage
- [Migrating to OptiView Live v2](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/roku/theolive/migrating-to-v2.md): If you are using OptiView Live v2 for your live streams, you will need to do some extra configuration to successfully play back your OptiView Live streams on Roku.
- [Token based security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/roku/theolive/token-based-security.md): OptiView Live offers the option to enable JWT token security on channel distribution level. This can be interesting if you only want valid users to access your stream. Read more about the feature and configuring it on your channels on the token based security guide.
- [How to detect active text track cues](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-detect-active-text-track-cues.md): This article describes how you can use the THEOplayer API to detect the active text track cues.
- [How to programmatically detect text track changes](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-detect-text-track-changes.md): This article describes how you can use the THEOplayer API to detect text track quality changes. A text track "change" is triggered by enabling (or disabling) a subtitle or closed captions track.
- [How to dynamically change the visible captions?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-dynamically-change-the-visible-captions.md): When working with the captions / text-track options, you can dynamically change the currently selected language (or set a default language) using following methods.
- [How to implement preview thumbnails in THEOplayer](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-implement-preview-thumbnails.md): This article describes how to enable preview thumbnails. In THEOplayer this is done with the TextTrack API.
- [How to insert subtitles?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-insert-subtitles.md): You may be asking this question if you need captions or subtitles on your video. The sourceDescription property textTracks can be used to add an array of side-loaded text tracks to the player. All valid tracks will be available for playback as long as the player’s source is not set again. In the following guide, you will find several related topics and some code samples for different SDKs.
- [How to programmatically detect text tracks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-programmatically-detect-text-tracks.md): This article describes how you can use the API to detect text tracks, which can be subtitles, closed captions or metadata.
- [How to programmatically enable or disable text tracks](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-programmatically-disable-text-tracks.md): This article describes how you can use the API to enable or disable subtitles, closed captions or metadata.
- [How to track ID3 cues / tags?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/how-to-track-id3-cues-tags.md): The question above is commonly asked by the developers who want to introduce a certain behavior depending on the metadata contained by the ID3 cues. For example, the developer wants:
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/texttrack/introduction.md): The articles in this section describe how to implement common use-cases related to text tracks, subtitles, closed captions and metadata. These use-cases can range from programmatically detecting new metadata, to side-loading subtitles.
- [Customizing the UI: vertical volume slider](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/customising-vertical-volume-slider.md): This article provides information regarding the implementation of a vertical volume slider instead of the default horizontal one.
- [Customize UI during ad playback](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/customize-ui-during-playback.md): This how-to guide describes how to customize the look and feel of the player when an advertisement is playing. We will be leveraging mainly some event listeners and CSS. In the section Resources, at the bottom of the page, you will also find links to the reference API and to a related how-to guide that explains when and how the texts displayed during ad playback (if any) may be changed.
- [How to align the timeline in the middle](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-align-timeline-in-the-middle.md): This question is asked when developers want to change the appearance of the current time digits and duration digits in the seekbar. For example, instead of having it aligned on the left of the control bar, they might want to have it in the middle.
- [How to build a Chromeless UI](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-build-chromeless-ui.md): A Chromeless UI comes into play when your envisioned video player UI too different from the default THEOplayer UI.
- [How to change the Big Play Button?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-change-big-play-button.md): The question above is typically asked by developers who want to increase/decrease the size, set a different image instead, or introduce on-hover behavior.
- [How to change the default UI language to other](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-change-default-UI-language-to-other.md): This article describes how to change the default UI language to other.
- [How to change how a video should be fit inside a container](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-change-how-a-video-should-fit-inside-a-container.md): This question is asked by developers who want to alter how a video should be sized inside the video's frame.
- [How to detect the visibility of the default control bar](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-detect-visibility-default-control-bar.md): This question is typically asked by developers that want to insert a custom element that should
- [How to disable click to pause](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-disable-click-to-pause.md): When you use the default UI of THEOplayer you can pause (and resume) the video by clicking anywhere on the video (except in the control bar).
- [How to insert a button](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-insert-a-button.md): This article provides information on how developers can add custom buttons to THEOplayer's default UI.
- [How to make an audio-only player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-make-audio-only-player.md): This article will tell you how to manipulate THEOplayer (Web) to make an audio-only interface, using only CSS and JavaScript. This applies also to mobile SDKs, where CSS and javascript can be injected (see Resources).
- [How to make the control bar always visible?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/how-to-make-control-keys-always-visible.md): The question above is typically asked by developers who want to be able to see the control bar at all times without having to continuously move the cursor over it (or tap the screen).
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/introduction.md): THEOplayer has a default user interface (UI). The default UI is optimized for browsers, mobile devices and tablets.
- [Is it possible to hide/disable the Google IMA UI?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/is-it-possible-to-hide-googla-ima.md): You may ask this question if you are trying to determine what controls are visible to the user during ands and want therefore to use your own UI elements.
- [Is it possible to remove an element from the UI?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/is-it-possible-to-remove-an-element.md): This question may be asked if you want to limit the options/controls that are showed in the UI.
- [UI Skinning](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/ui/ui-skinning.md): THEOplayer allows you to style the player to your own needs. This guide will give an in-depth explanation of which colors can be changed and which parts of the player are affected. First it will explain how to change the skin of your player using 6 custom CSS classes. Afterwards, an overview follows explaining which parts of the players are affected by each class. If you don't already have an up-and-running version of THEOplayer, take a look at our getting started guide.
- [Getting started with CMCD on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/cmcd/getting-started.md): Request Mode
- [MPEG-DASH Content Steering](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/content-steering/mpeg-dash-content-steering.md): This guide shows you how to use MPEG-DASH Content Steering in the THEOplayer Web SDK.
- [Basic Playback Guide](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/basic-playback-guide.md): After following our Getting Started on Web guide or the Player Setup section below, you will be ready to programmatically perform common playback actions. These include increasing/decreasing the volume, playing/pausing the stream, enabling full screen, and selecting quality layers.
- [Airplay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/casting/airplay.md): OptiView Live streams have support for Airplay. However, a limitation for now is that the latency is higher while casting (about 12 seconds). Configuring it is exactly the same as for other streams you would play with THEOplayer. The instructions are described in the Airplay guide.
- [Chromecast](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/casting/chromecast.md): OptiView Live streams have support for Chromecast. However, a limitation for now is that the latency is higher while casting (about 12 seconds). Configuring it is exactly the same as for other streams you would play with THEOplayer. The instructions are described in the Chromecast guide.
- [Getting started](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/getting-started.md): Usage
- [Metadata](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/metadata.md): Metadata that travels through your OptiView Live stream — whether pushed via the API or embedded as SEI — surfaces on the player as cues on a text track. The general approach is the same in every case:
- [Migrating from deprecated THEOlive player to THEOplayer on Web](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/migrating-from-theolive-player.md): The goal of this guide is to help you migrate from the deprecated theolive player to the recommended THEOplayer. If you haven't integrated with any player, please follow the recommended guide instead of this one.
- [Token based security](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/theolive/token-based-security.md): OptiView Live offers the option to enable JWT token security on a distribution (formerly referred to as an alias) level. This can be interesting if you only want valid users to access your stream. Read more about the feature and configuring it on your channels on the token based security guide.
- [Uplynk Ads](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/ads.md): Uplynk offers support for server-side ad-insertion (SSAI).
- [Uplynk configuration](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/configuration.md): This article explains what is possible with an Uplynk configuration when setting up a THEOplayer instance.
- [Uplynk interface](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/interface.md): This article explains how developers can hook into the Uplynk interface. This interface allows developers to query the available assets and ads in an Uplynk stream, its properties, and subscribe to its events.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/introduction.md): THEOplayer and Uplynk are partners in the streaming industry. Uplynk offers services like encoding, transcoding, server-side ad-insertion, a CDN and more.
- [Ping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/ping.md): This article explains how to use Uplynk's Ping API.
- [Preplay](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/how-to-guides/web/uplynk/preplay.md): This article explains how to leverage the Uplynk Preplay API through THEOplayer. This API allows developers to pre-integrate with Uplynk's Preplay service.
- [OptiView Player iOS & tvOS SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/ios.md)
- [Knowledge base](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base.md): Want to know more about topics in the video industry? We have a small list of articles to provide you some basic knowledge.
- [Advertisements](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/advertisement/introduction.md): This section allows you to learn everything about advertisements!
- [Server Side Ad Insertion](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/advertisement/server-side-ad-insertion.md): If you are a content distributor, you know how difficult it is to properly monetize content. While content acquisition can be expensive, more and more users are looking for ways to access premium content for free.
- [Support matrix](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/advertisement/support-matrix.md): THEOplayer currently supports two ad implementations:
- [Advertising User Guide](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/advertisement/user-guide.md): THEOplayer allows you to configure different aspects of its advertising environment. By following this guide you will learn about:
- [AES-128 Encryption](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/content-protection/aes-128-encryption.md): The Apple HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) specification uses AES-128 encryption to provide content protection. The Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is a specification for the encryption of electronic data established by the U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology. In case of HLS with AES-128, individual media segments get encrypted with a 16-octet key. Most modern streaming servers have simple configuration options for enabling AES-128 on HLS streams.
- [Digital Rights Management (DRM) Systems](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/content-protection/drm-systems.md): Intellectual property theft has a huge economic impact. The digital media sector is one of its biggest victims with annual losses running into billions of dollars.
- [Content protection](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/content-protection/introduction.md): Get ready to learn everything about content protection.
- [Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/cors/introduction.md): Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) is a content protection mechanism that allows resources (manifests, video files or encryption keys) on server to be requested from a webpage on a different domain, subdomain or port than the one on from which the resources originated.
- [Timed Metadata](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/metadata/introduction.md): Timed metadata describes a subset of a stream through data (bytes, text, image, ...), a start time and/or a stop time.
- [Basic guide to Web Audio API](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/basic-guide-web-audio-api.md): THEOplayer provides a Web Audio API, which allows you to do some quite nifty stuff with the player's audio output. If you are unfamiliar with the ideas behind Web Audio API, Mozilla has an excellent intro to the basic ideas you need to know about here. This guide will help you set up a demo just like the one we have in our Demo Zone.
- [Frame Accurate Seeking](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/frame-accurate-seeking.md): This guide will explain how you can achieve frame-accurate seeking with THEOplayer. It will explain how to calculate the current frame of your player, how to seek to a specific frame and known limitations with this approach.
- [Playback](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/introduction.md): This section will update you about the world of playback.
- [Program Date Time](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/program-date-time.md): THEOplayer has support for associating media segments with an absolute date and time. This can be useful when you want to synchronize video playback with displaying other relevant information about the video stream.
- [Chapter markers](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/subtitles-and-closed-captions/chapter-markers.md): This guide explains to you how to set up THEOplayer to display chapter markers as a way of navigating videos. For more information regarding chapter markers, see Resources at the bottom of this page.
- [Introduction](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/subtitles-and-closed-captions/introduction.md): THEOplayer supports rendering of subtitles and closed captions in both live and VOD content via the WebVTT format. You can supply subtitles and closed captions to THEOplayer in WebVTT format either externally via the `` element or embedded inside the HLS stream.
- [Video Clipping](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/video-clipping.md): THEOplayer supports playing only a selected part of a video-on-demand stream, rather than playing it from start to finish. This can be used for example to watch a single game-changing event from a sports match, highlight an important part of a long video presentation, or skip straight to the best part of a song's video clip.
- [What are chapter markers?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/what-are-chapter-markers.md): Dividing a video into separate parts helps your audience navigate through your content. While hovering the seek bar, THEOplayer's chapter markers and titles will help your users identify different parts of your content more easily. It also allows them to revisit their favorite parts of your videos.
- [What are the benefits of preview thumbnails?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/playback/what-are-the-benefits-of-preview-thumbnails.md): Preview thumbnails improve the user experience by allowing the user to scan the video for specific content. They also have a business impact determined by the bandwidth optimization connected to users that can decide and quickly jump to the content they are interested in.
- [HTTP Live Streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/streaming/http-live-streaming.md): HTTP Live Streaming (also known as HLS) is a protocol implemented by Apple for streaming of audio and/or video content over HTTP. HLS has become over the last years a de facto standard for streaming content online. The main reasons for this are:
- [Streaming](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/streaming/introduction.md): Always wanted to know more about streaming? This is your chance!
- [What are the recommended stream settings?](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/knowledge-base/streaming/what-are-the-recommended-stream-settings.md): There are many options to encode your streams. These encoding are often related to the content you are streaming. For example, fast motion movies like sporting events might require higher frame rates and more bandwidth compared to video of static landscape webcams.
- [OptiView Player React Native SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/react-native.md)
- [OptiView Player Roku SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/roku.md)
- [OptiView Player Web SDK](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/web.md)
- [OptiView Hosted Player](/documentation/pr-preview/pr-690/theoplayer/web/hosted-player.md): Dolby hosts a special version of the OptiView video player for use with customers intended to be used for `` embedding. This version of the player is configurable with query parameters to make it easy to setup and configure without having to host the player yourself.
